V021-N00003.label: 儿科
V021-N00003.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00004.label: 丁奚疳
V021-N00004.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00004.definitions: a severe case of infantile malnutrition with an emaciated T-shaped figure

V021-N00005.label: 五硬
V021-N00005.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00005.englishNames: five stiffnesses; five stiffness; five kinds of stiffness
V021-N00005.definitions: stiffness of the hand, foot, waist, flesh and neck in the newborn due to a congenital defect

V021-N00006.label: 五软
V021-N00006.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00006.englishNames: five limpnesses/ flaccidity; five kinds of flaccidity
V021-N00006.definitions: flaccidity of the neck, nape, extremities, muscles and mastication as striking features of delayed growth and mental retardation in infants

V021-N00007.label: 五迟
V021-N00007.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00007.englishNames: five retardations; Five Kinds of Retardation
V021-N00007.definitions: retarded development in infants covering standing, walking, hair-growth, tooth eruption and speaking

V021-N00008.label: 内钓
V021-N00008.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00008.englishNames: convulsion with abdominal pain; convulsions with visceral colic
V021-N00008.definitions: infantile convulsion mainly manifested as visceral contraction and abdominal colic

V021-N00009.label: 厌食
V021-N00009.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00009.definitions: a diseased state in children characterized by loss of appetite for food

V021-N00010.label: 变蒸
V021-N00010.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00010.definitions: a physiological phenomenon due to the growth and development of the child

V021-N00011.label: 口疮
V021-N00011.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00011.aliases: 口疮; 口疳; 口糜; Oral Sores

V021-N00012.label: 哺乳疳
V021-N00012.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00012.definitions: malnutrition of an infant due to improper breastfeeding

V021-N00013.label: 喉痧
V021-N00013.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00013.aliases: 烂喉丹痧; 丹痧; 疫痧
V021-N00013.definitions: an acute seasonal infectious disease marked by swelling and erosion of the throat, erythematous rash and raspberry tongue

V021-N00014.label: 囟填
V021-N00014.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00014.definitions: outward swelling of the fontanel in an infant

V021-N00015.label: 囟陷
V021-N00015.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00016.label: 夜啼
V021-N00016.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00016.definitions: morbid night crying in babies, but quiet at daytime

V021-N00017.label: 天钓
V021-N00017.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00017.englishNames: convulsion with uplifted eyes; convulsions with uplifted eyes
V021-N00017.definitions: infantile convulsion with high fever, tossed head and upward staring eyes

V021-N00018.label: 客忤
V021-N00018.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00018.definitions: a seizure of fright that causes vomiting, abdominal pain, and even convulsions

V021-N00019.label: 客忤夜啼
V021-N00019.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00019.definitions: night crying in babies caused by fright

V021-N00020.label: 小儿哮喘
V021-N00020.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00020.definitions: an infantile disease characterized by paroxysmal labored breathing accompanied by a whistling sound

V021-N00021.label: 干疳
V021-N00021.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00021.englishNames: dryness (infantile) malnutrition; dryness infantile malnutrition; chronic infantile malnutrition
V021-N00021.definitions: a case of infantile malnutrition at the extreme stage with drying up of fluid and blood, marked by a dry and shriveled body worn

V021-N00022.label: 心疳
V021-N00022.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00022.definitions: a case of infantile malnutrition due to debilitation of the spleen and stomach with depressed heat in the heart meridian, the same as fright malnutrition

V021-N00023.label: 急惊风
V021-N00023.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00023.definitions: infantile convulsion of acute onset, accompanied by high fever and loss of consciousness

V021-N00024.label: 惊痫
V021-N00024.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00025.label: 惊风
V021-N00025.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00025.definitions: infantile diseases marked by convulsions and loss of consciousness

V021-N00026.label: 慢惊风
V021-N00026.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00026.definitions: repeated infantile convulsion of gradual onset, associated with loss of consciousness or paralysis and poor prognosis

V021-N00027.label: 慢脾风
V021-N00027.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00027.englishNames: chronic spleen wind; chronic convulsion due to spleen disorder
V021-N00027.definitions: a critical case of chronic infantile convulsion marked by yang debilitation and yin exuberance, usually occurring after protracted vomiting and diarrhea with spleen qi deficiency and stirring of liver wind

V021-N00028.label: 木舌
V021-N00028.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00028.definitions: swollen, hardened tongue, stiff as a piece of wood, seen in glossitis of the newborn

V021-N00029.label: 水痘
V021-N00029.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00029.aliases: 水痘; 水疱; 水花; 水疮
V021-N00029.definitions: an acute contagious disease caused by seasonal toxin and manifested by fever and bursts of macular and papular eruptions followed by incrustation but no scar formation, the same as varicella

V021-N00030.label: 溢乳
V021-N00030.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00030.definitions: vomiting of milk due to improper feeding

V021-N00031.label: 滞颐
V021-N00031.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00031.englishNames: dribbling; Frequently Drool in Infancy; Infantile Slobbering with Saliva Wetting the Lower Jaw
V021-N00031.definitions: salivation in an infant with wet cheeks

V021-N00032.label: 疰夏
V021-N00032.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00032.aliases: 疰夏; 夏季热; Summer Nonacclimation
V021-N00032.definitions: children’s disease usually occurring in summer, with symptoms of lassitude, dyspepsia, and abnormal bowel movements

V021-N00033.label: 疳
V021-N00033.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00033.definitions: a chronic nutritional disorder of infants with emaciation and weakness

V021-N00034.label: 疳气
V021-N00034.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00034.englishNames: mild (infantile) malnutrition; mild infantile malnutrition; gan qi
V021-N00034.definitions: a mild case of infantile malnutrition at the early stage

V021-N00035.label: 疳积
V021-N00035.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00035.englishNames: mild (infantile) malnutrition with accumulation; infantile malnutrition with accumulation; infantile malnutrition
V021-N00035.definitions: infantile malnutrition at the intermediate stage, accompanied by food accumulation and stagnation in the stomach and intestines

V021-N00036.label: 疳肿胀
V021-N00036.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00036.definitions: infantile malnutrition marked by edema and abdominal distention

V021-N00037.label: 痄腮
V021-N00037.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00037.definitions: an epidemic disease caused by wind-heat toxin and characterized by painful swelling of one or both parotid glands

V021-N00038.label: 痘疮
V021-N00038.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00038.definitions: an acute highly contagious febrile disease characterized by skin eruptions with pustules, sloughing, and scar formation, the same as variola

V021-N00039.label: 痧
V021-N00039.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00039.englishNames: exanthem; rash of measles; filthy-attack disease
V021-N00039.definitions: a general term for skin eruption or rash, but usually referring to measles

V021-N00040.label: 痰痫
V021-N00040.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00040.definitions: epileptic seizure induced by depressed qi and bound phlegm

V021-N00041.label: 癫痫
V021-N00041.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00041.aliases: 癫痫; 癫疾; 痫病; 痫证
V021-N00041.definitions: a disease characterized by temporary loss of consciousness with foaming at the mouth and convulsions of the limbs, and return to normal after the episode

V021-N00042.label: 白喉
V021-N00042.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00042.definitions: an acute infectious disease characterized by fever, sore throat and the formation of white false membrane attached firmly to the pharynx, larynx and nose

V021-N00043.label: 百晬内嗽
V021-N00043.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00043.definitions: cough with sputum and dyspnea occurring in a newborn within one hundred days after birth

V021-N00044.label: 眼疳
V021-N00044.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00044.definitions: attack of liver fire to the eyes in a malnourished child

V021-N00045.label: 肝疳
V021-N00045.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00045.englishNames: liver (infantile) malnutrition; infantile malnutrition involving liver
V021-N00045.definitions: a case of infantile malnutrition due to debilitation of the spleen and stomach with depressed heat in the liver meridian, the same as sinew malnutrition

V021-N00046.label: 肺疳
V021-N00046.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00046.englishNames: lung (infantile) malnutrition; infantile malnutrition involving lung
V021-N00046.definitions: a case of infantile malnutrition due to debilitation of the spleen and stomach with depressed heat damaging the lung, the same as qi malnutrition

V021-N00047.label: 肾疳
V021-N00047.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00047.englishNames: kidney (infantile) malnutrition; infantile malnutrition involving kidney
V021-N00047.definitions: a case of infantile malnutrition ascribed to a congenital defect with weak constitution or chronic spleen disorder that involves the kidney, resulting in dual deficiency of the spleen and kidney, the same as bone malnutrition

V021-N00048.label: 胎怯
V021-N00048.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00048.aliases: 胎怯; 胎弱; fetus convulsion

V021-N00049.label: 胎热
V021-N00049.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00049.definitions: a group of diseases with heat manifestations occurring in the newborn due to contraction of heat toxin in the fetal stage

V021-N00050.label: 胎赤
V021-N00050.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00050.definitions: abnormal redness of the skin in a newborn, often due to affection by toxic heat at the fetal stage, the same as erythroderma neonatorum

V021-N00051.label: 胎黄
V021-N00051.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00051.definitions: yellow discoloration of the skin and sclera seen in the newborn

V021-N00052.label: 脐湿
V021-N00052.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00052.definitions: a condition of wetness of and possible exudation from the umbilicus after the umbilical cord has been shed, referring to omphalorrhea

V021-N00053.label: 脐疝
V021-N00053.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00053.definitions: a type of abdominal hernia in which part of the intestine protrudes at the umbilicus and is covered with skin, sometimes red and swollen

V021-N00054.label: 脐疮
V021-N00054.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00054.definitions: redness and swelling of the umbilicus or ulceration spreading around the umbilicus with purulent exudation

V021-N00055.label: 脐风
V021-N00055.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00055.definitions: a disease of the newborn characterized by cyanotic lips, lockjaw, generalized tonic spasm and tetanic convulsions, referring to neonatal tetanus

V021-N00056.label: 脾疳
V021-N00056.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00056.englishNames: spleen (infantile) malnutrition; infantile malnutrition involving spleen
V021-N00056.definitions: a case of infantile malnutrition due to debilitation of the spleen and stomach with accumulation of dampness-heat, the same as food malnutrition

V021-N00057.label: 蛔疳
V021-N00057.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00057.englishNames: ascaris (infantile) malnutrition; infantile malnutrition due to ascariasis

V021-N00058.label: 血疳
V021-N00058.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00058.definitions: a case of infantile malnutrition marked by blood deficiency

V021-N00059.label: 解颅
V021-N00059.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00059.englishNames: ununited skull; ununited skull(hydrocephalus); hydrocephalus due to nonclosure of fontanel
V021-N00059.definitions: abnormal accumulation of excess fluid within the skull marked by enlargement of the head and retarded closure of the skull sutures, the same as hydrocephalus

V021-N00060.label: 顿咳
V021-N00060.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00060.definitions: an acute contagious infection of the respiratory system with characteristic paroxysmal cough, consisting of a deep inspiration, followed by a series of quick, short coughs which end with a long shrill and whooping inspiration, referring to pertussis

V021-N00061.label: 风疹
V021-N00061.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00061.aliases: 风疹; 风痧; 风痧(病)
V021-N00061.definitions: a contagious eruptive disease caused by seasonal wind-heat or wind-heat toxin, and marked by mild fever, followed by enlargement of postauricular and suboccipital lymph nodes, and the appearance of pink maculo-papular rash that begins on the head and

V021-N00062.label: 风痫
V021-N00062.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00062.definitions: epileptic seizure induced by liver wind stirring internally

V021-N00063.label: 食积
V021-N00063.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00063.definitions: a diseased state characterized by the accumulation of undigested food in the stomach and intestines, causing abdominal distension and pain, vomiting, diarrhea and anorexia

V021-N00064.label: 马牙
V021-N00064.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00064.definitions: sporadic eruption of small yellowish spots on the gums in the newborn

V021-N00065.label: 马脾风
V021-N00065.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00065.definitions: critical case of asthma and lung distension in children with a sudden attack

V021-N00066.label: 鹅口疮
V021-N00066.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00066.definitions: a disease marked by white patches on the buccal mucosa and tongue

V021-N00067.label: 麻疹
V021-N00067.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00067.definitions: an acute epidemic eruptive disease marked by fever and generalized maculopapular rash preceded by cough, nasal discharge, profuse tearing, and white speckles inside the mouth

V021-N00068.label: 齘齿
V021-N00068.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00068.definitions: noisy grinding of teeth during sleep

V021-N00069.label: 龟背
V021-N00069.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00069.definitions: back shaped like a tortoise-shell due to kyphosis

V021-N00070.label: 龟胸
V021-N00070.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00070.definitions: deformity of the chest in which the sternum is prominent, like the chicken breast, also known as pectus carinatum

V021-N00071.label: 内科
V021-N00071.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00072.label: 外科
V021-N00072.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00073.label: 丘疹
V021-N00073.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00073.definitions: small conical elevation of the skin

V021-N00074.label: 丹毒
V021-N00074.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00074.aliases: 丹毒; BWC080; 火丹; 抱头火丹; 流火; 内发丹毒
V021-N00074.englishNames: erysipelas; Inflammatory Disease With Redness Of The Skin
V021-N00074.definitions: an acute infection of the skin marked by intense local redness

V021-N00075.label: 乳发
V021-N00075.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00075.aliases: 乳发; BWR030; 脱壳乳痈
V021-N00075.englishNames: phlegmonous mastitis; Suppurative Mastitis; suppurative mastitis
V021-N00075.definitions: a serious suppurative inflammation of the breast leading to necrosis and abscess formation

V021-N00076.label: 乳头风
V021-N00076.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00076.aliases: 乳头风; BWP075; 乳头破碎; 奶头风
V021-N00076.englishNames: cracked nipple; Cracked Nipple; nipple wind; rhagadia mammae
V021-N00076.definitions: painful fissuring of the mammary nipple or areola

V021-N00077.label: 乳岩
V021-N00077.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00077.englishNames: rocky mass in the breast/breast cancer; Breast  Carcinoma; carcinoma of breast
V021-N00077.definitions: malignant tumor occurring in the mammary region

V021-N00078.label: 乳漏
V021-N00078.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00078.definitions: an abnormal passage that leads to the cutaneous surface of the breast or areola of the nipple and exudes pus

V021-N00079.label: 乳疬
V021-N00079.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00079.englishNames: gynecomastia; hypertrophy of breast; mastauxy in children
V021-N00079.definitions: excessive development of the breast in the children and male adults

V021-N00080.label: 乳痈
V021-N00080.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00080.aliases: 乳痈; BWR020; Deep Rooted Breast Carbuncles
V021-N00080.definitions: acute pyogenic inflammation of the breast

V021-N00081.label: 乳痨
V021-N00081.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00081.englishNames: mammary phthisis; Mammary Tuberculosis; tuberculosis of breast

V021-N00082.label: 乳癖
V021-N00082.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00082.englishNames: mammary hyperplasia; lump in breast; breast lump(hyperplasia of mammary gland)

V021-N00083.label: 乳衄
V021-N00083.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00083.englishNames: thelorrhagia; Bleeding from the Breast; Bleeding from the Nipple; bleeding of nipple (thelorrhagia)

V021-N00084.label: 内吹乳痈
V021-N00084.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00084.definitions: inflammation of the breast occurring during pregnancy

V021-N00085.label: 内痔
V021-N00085.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00085.englishNames: internal hemorrhoid; Interal Piles; Internal Hemorrhoids
V021-N00085.definitions: varicose dilatation of a vein of the superior hemorrhoidal plexus, situated proximal to the pectinate line

V021-N00086.label: 冻疮
V021-N00086.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00086.aliases: 冻疮; BWV000; 冻风; Chilblain; 冻僵; 冻烂疮; 冻死; 水浸手; 水浸足
V021-N00086.definitions: a lesion marked by swelling, numbness, cyanosis, blister or ulcerating sore attributed to exposure to cold that causes local stagnation of qi and blood

V021-N00087.label: 发
V021-N00087.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00087.definitions: acute, diffuse and suppurative inflammation of the subcutaneous tissue

V021-N00088.label: 发际疮

V021-N00095.label: 头痈

V021-N00096.label: 委中毒

V021-N00100.label: 息肉
V021-N00100.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00101.label: 息肉痔
V021-N00101.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00101.englishNames: polyp of rectum; Polyp  Hemorrhoids; Polypoid  Hemorrhoid; rectal polyp

V021-N00102.label: 手发背

V021-N00119.label: 疖

V021-N00162.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00162.definitions: a type of erysipelas in the newborn characterized by changing the location of the lesion either from the trunk to the extremities or from the extremities to the trunk

V021-N00163.label: 足发背
V021-N00163.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00164.label: 附骨疽
V021-N00164.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00164.aliases: 附骨疽; BWC131; 多骨疽; 咬骨疽; 朽骨疽
V021-N00164.englishNames: suppurative osteomyelitis; Suppurative Osteomyelitis; bone-attaching carbuncle(suppurative osteomyelitis)
V021-N00164.definitions: a suppurative inflammatory disease of bone, marked by local death and separation of tissue

V021-N00165.label: 青蛇毒
V021-N00165.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00165.aliases: 青蛇毒; BWC210; 䐔病; 赤脉; 恶脉; 黄鳅痈
V021-N00165.englishNames: green-blue snake toxin sore; Thrombophlebitis; acute thrombotic phlebitis; acute thrombophlebitis
V021-N00165.definitions: inflammation of a superficial vein associated with thrombus formation, referring to superficial thrombophlebitis

V021-N00166.label: 面游风
V021-N00166.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00166.aliases: 面游风; BWP190; 白屑风
V021-N00166.englishNames: seborrheic dermatitis; Seborrheic  Dermatitis; Seborrheic Eczema; facial seborrheic dermatitis; facial wandering wind
V021-N00166.definitions: a chronic inflammation of the skin marked by excessive secretion of sebum

V021-N00167.label: 骨瘤
V021-N00167.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00167.englishNames: bone tumor; Bone  Tumour; Osteoma; stony tumor sticking to bone; osteoma
V021-N00167.definitions: tumor of the bone, either benign or malignant

V021-N00168.label: 鸡眼
V021-N00168.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00168.englishNames: corn; Clavus; Corn; Heloma; Helosis; clavus
V021-N00168.definitions: a horny thickening of the skin, forming a conical mass pointing down into the corium (dermis)

V021-N00169.label: 鹅掌风
V021-N00169.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00169.englishNames: tinea manuum; Gooseweb Wind; goose-web wind(tinea manuum); gooseweb wind
V021-N00169.definitions: ringworm affecting the hands, referring to chronic dermatitis with keratosis

V021-N00170.label: 妇产科
V021-N00170.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00171.label: 不孕
V021-N00171.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00171.definitions: lack of capacity to produce offspring

V021-N00172.label: 临产
V021-N00172.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00172.definitions: the process of giving birth to offspring, including the expulsion of the fetus, placenta and membranes from the uterus through the vagina

V021-N00173.label: 乳汁不行
V021-N00173.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00174.label: 乳汁自出
V021-N00174.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00174.aliases: 乳汁自出; 乳溢; 产后乳汁自出
V021-N00174.definitions: spontaneous flow of milk irrespective of nursing

V021-N00175.label: 五不女
V021-N00175.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00176.label: 产后乳汁自出
V021-N00176.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00176.definitions: spontaneous flow of milk irrespective of nursing following childbirth

V021-N00177.label: 产褥
V021-N00177.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00177.definitions: the period between childbirth and the return of the uterus to its normal condition

V021-N00178.label: 伤产
V021-N00178.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00178.definitions: premature delivery due to traumatic injury

V021-N00179.label: 倒经
V021-N00179.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00179.definitions: discharge of blood from some part other than the vagina during the menstrual period, such as bleeding from the nose or vomiting of blood

V021-N00180.label: 堕胎
V021-N00180.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00180.aliases: 堕胎; 胎堕不全; 胎殒难留; 早期流产; 坠胎
V021-N00180.definitions: spontaneous abortion occurring within the first twelve weeks of pregnancy

V021-N00181.label: 子嗽
V021-N00181.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00181.definitions: persistent cough during pregnancy, the same as gravid cough

V021-N00182.label: 子悬
V021-N00182.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00182.definitions: feeling of pressure in the abdomen and thorax, even with dyspnea and irritability, during pregnancy, the same as gravid oppression or upward flow of fetus qi

V021-N00183.label: 子晕
V021-N00183.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00183.definitions: dizziness with blurred vision or even fainting occurring in pregnancy, the same as gravid vertigo

V021-N00184.label: 子死腹中
V021-N00184.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00184.aliases: 子死腹中; 死产; 死产不下
V021-N00184.englishNames: dead fetus in the uterus; Dead Foetus in the Uterus
V021-N00184.definitions: retention of a dead fetus in the uterus beyond the period of normal gestation

V021-N00185.label: 子淋
V021-N00185.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00185.definitions: difficult and painful discharge of urine during pregnancy

V021-N00186.label: 子满
V021-N00186.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00186.definitions: that causing abnormally enlarged abdomen, sensation of fullness and dyspnea

V021-N00187.label: 子烦
V021-N00187.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00187.definitions: depression or irritability occurring during pregnancy

V021-N00188.label: 子痫
V021-N00188.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00188.aliases: 子痫; 妊娠痫证; 子冒
V021-N00188.definitions: sudden onset of convulsions and loss of consciousness occurring in a pregnant or puerperal woman with headache and vertigo

V021-N00189.label: 子肿
V021-N00189.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00189.definitions: edema of the face and limbs occurring in the late stage of pregnancy

V021-N00190.label: 小产
V021-N00190.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00190.aliases: 小产; 流产; 晚期流产
V021-N00190.definitions: spontaneous abortion occurring after the twelfth week and before the twenty-eighth week of pregnancy

V021-N00191.label: 居经
V021-N00191.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00191.definitions: menstruation occurring once every three months, but causing no symptoms

V021-N00192.label: 崩漏
V021-N00192.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00192.aliases: 崩漏; 暴崩; 崩中; 漏下; 血崩; Uterine Bleeding
V021-N00192.definitions: sudden onset of profuse uterine bleeding or incessant dripping of blood, occurring not in the regular menstruation period, the same as metrorrhagia

V021-N00193.label: 带下
V021-N00193.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00193.aliases: 带下; 带下病; 带下过多; 带下过少; Morbid Leukorrhea
V021-N00193.definitions: (1) normal discharge from the vagina; (2) diseases characterized by discharge from the vagina, the same as leukorrhea

V021-N00194.label: 并月
V021-N00194.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00194.definitions: menstruation occurring once every two months, but causing no symptoms

V021-N00195.label: 弄胎
V021-N00195.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00195.definitions: intermittent abdominal pain that occurs toward the end of the term of pregnancy without backache, also known as agitated fetus

V021-N00196.label: 恶阻
V021-N00196.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00196.definitions: nausea and vomiting during early pregnancy

V021-N00197.label: 恶露
V021-N00197.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00198.label: 恶露不下
V021-N00198.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00199.label: 恶露不绝
V021-N00199.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00199.aliases: 恶露不绝; 恶露不止; Prolonged Lochia
V021-N00199.englishNames: persistent flow of the lochia; Lochiorrhea
V021-N00199.definitions: abnormally prolonged discharge of lochia for more than 3 weeks after childbirth, the same as lochiorrhea

V021-N00200.label: 月经不调
V021-N00200.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00200.definitions: a general term for irregular menstruation and other menstrual complaints, such as abnormal duration, amount, color and quality of menstrual discharge

V021-N00201.label: 月经先后无定期
V021-N00201.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00201.aliases: 月经先后无定期; 经乱; Irrgularity of Menstrual Cycle; 经水先后无定期; 月经愆期
V021-N00201.englishNames: menstruation at irregular intervals; Irregular Menstrual Cycle
V021-N00201.pinyinNames: yue jing xian hou wu ding qi
V021-N00201.definitions: periods that come with an irregular cycle, more than one week early or later

V021-N00202.label: 月经先期
V021-N00202.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00202.aliases: 月经先期; 经行先期; 经早; 经期超前
V021-N00202.englishNames: advanced menstruation; Menstruation Ahead of the Time; Preceded Menstrual Cycle
V021-N00202.definitions: periods that come one week or more ahead of due time, for more than two successive periods

V021-N00203.label: 月经后期
V021-N00203.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00203.aliases: 月经后期; 经行后期; 经迟; 月经落后; 月经延后
V021-N00203.definitions: periods that come one week or more after due time, for more than two successive periods

V021-N00204.label: 月经病
V021-N00204.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00204.definitions: a collective term for diseases characterized by abnormal interval, duration, quantity, color, quality of menses, non-physiological stoppage, as well as manifest symptoms with the periods or around menopause, the same as emmeniopathy

V021-N00205.label: 月经过多
V021-N00205.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00206.label: 月经过少
V021-N00206.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00206.aliases: 月经过少; 月经涩少
V021-N00206.definitions: menstrual discharge of less than the normal amount occurring at regular intervals

V021-N00207.label: 滑胎
V021-N00207.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00207.aliases: 滑胎; 数堕胎; 习惯性流产
V021-N00207.definitions: spontaneous abortion in three or more consecutive pregnancies

V021-N00208.label: 漏下
V021-N00208.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00208.definitions: slight but persistent leakage of blood from the uterus, the same as metrostaxis

V021-N00209.label: 激经
V021-N00209.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00209.definitions: regular menstruation during early pregnancy, which stops spontaneously when the fetus is fully developed

V021-N00210.label: 痛经
V021-N00210.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00210.aliases: 痛经; 月经痛; 经行腹痛
V021-N00210.definitions: lower abdominal pain or referring pain to the lower back occurring around or during the menstrual period

V021-N00211.label: 白带
V021-N00211.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00211.definitions: (1) whitish discharge from the vagina; (2) a disease marked by white discharge from the vagina

V021-N00212.label: 石瘕
V021-N00212.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00212.englishNames: stony conglomeration; stony uterine mass; Strong Uterine Mass
V021-N00212.definitions: a mass as hard as stone formed in the uterus

V021-N00213.label: 经期延长
V021-N00213.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00213.aliases: 经期延长; 经事延长
V021-N00213.definitions: excessively prolonged menstruation in regular cycles, the same as menostaxis

V021-N00214.label: 经行乳房胀痛
V021-N00214.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00214.englishNames: distending pain in the breasts during menstruation; Distending  Pain of Breasts During Menstruation
V021-N00214.pinyinNames: jing xing ru fang zhang tong
V021-N00214.definitions: distention or sensation of fullness and pain in the breasts before, during, or after menstruation

V021-N00215.label: 经行情志异常
V021-N00215.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00215.englishNames: moodiness during menstruation; Moody State  during Menstruation
V021-N00215.pinyinNames: jing xing qing zhi yi chang
V021-N00215.definitions: depression, gloominess, irritability and other changes of mood occurring around each menstrual period and returning to normal after the period

V021-N00216.label: 经间期出血
V021-N00216.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00216.aliases: 经间期出血; 排卵期出血
V021-N00216.definitions: uterine bleeding occurring not in the regular menstruation period

V021-N00217.label: 缺乳
V021-N00217.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00217.aliases: 缺乳; 产后缺乳; 乳汁不行; 乳汁不足
V021-N00217.definitions: deficiency of milk secretion during lactation, the same as hypogalactia

V021-N00218.label: 肠覃
V021-N00218.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00218.englishNames: lower abdominal mass (in woman); Ferale Abdominal Mass
V021-N00218.definitions: formation of mass in the lower abdomen with regular menstrual cycle

V021-N00219.label: 胎动不安
V021-N00219.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00219.definitions: continuous moving of the fetus with lumbar pain and abdominal pain or small amount of vaginal bleeding

V021-N00220.label: 胎漏
V021-N00220.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00220.aliases: 胎漏; 胞漏; 漏胎; 先兆流产
V021-N00220.definitions: small amount of blood discharge through vagina during pregnancy, intermittent, but with no lumbar pain or abdominal pain

V021-N00221.label: 胞衣不下
V021-N00221.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00222.label: 胞阻
V021-N00222.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00222.definitions: lower abdominal pain occurring in pregnancy, usually due to the impeded flow of qi and blood in uterine vessels

V021-N00223.label: 脏躁
V021-N00223.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00223.definitions: a paroxysmal mental disease marked by depression and melancholy, irritability, lack of control over emotions, sadness with an urge to weep, and sighing for no apparent reason

V021-N00224.label: 血崩
V021-N00224.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00224.definitions: sudden massive uterine bleeding, the same as metrorrhagia

V021-N00225.label: 试水
V021-N00225.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00225.definitions: the condition characterized by leakage of the amniotic fluid which is not followed by childbirth

V021-N00226.label: 试胎
V021-N00226.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00226.definitions: abdominal pain that occurs for a short time and then stops, occurring during the eighth or ninth month of pregnancy

V021-N00227.label: 转胞
V021-N00227.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00227.definitions: a disease marked by acute pain below the umbilicus associated with anuresis

V021-N00228.label: 过期不产
V021-N00228.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00228.aliases: 过期不产; 过期妊娠; 滞产
V021-N00228.definitions: pregnancy extended beyond the expected date of delivery

V021-N00229.label: 避年
V021-N00229.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00229.definitions: menstruation occurring once a year, but causing no symptoms

V021-N00230.label: 闭经
V021-N00230.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00230.aliases: 闭经; 经闭; 避年居经
V021-N00230.definitions: failure of menstruation to occur at puberty or abnormal stoppage of the menses for more than three months after menarche

V021-N00231.label: 阴吹
V021-N00231.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00231.definitions: noisy expulsion of gas from the vagina

V021-N00232.label: 阴挺
V021-N00232.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00232.aliases: 阴挺; 阴脱; 阴菌; 子宫脱出; 子宫脱垂
V021-N00232.englishNames: prolapse of the uterus; uterine prolapse
V021-N00232.definitions: downward displacement of the uterus, even with the entire uterus outside the vaginal orifice

V021-N00233.label: 阴疮
V021-N00233.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00233.aliases: 阴疮; 非特异性外阴溃疡; 前庭大腺炎脓肿破溃; 外阴肿瘤继发感染; 阴蚀
V021-N00233.definitions: sore or ulcer in the female external genital region

V021-N00234.label: 阴痒
V021-N00234.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00234.aliases: 阴痒; 外阴瘙痒症; 外阴炎; 外阴营养不良; 阴道炎; 阴门瘙痒; 阴（匿虫）
V021-N00234.definitions: itching of the female external genitalia or vagina

V021-N00235.label: 阴痛
V021-N00235.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00235.aliases: 阴痛; 嫁痛; 小户嫁痛; 阴户痛; 阴中痛
V021-N00235.definitions: pain of the female pudendum, including the vestibule of the vagina and vaginal orifice

V021-N00236.label: 阴肿
V021-N00236.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00236.aliases: 阴肿; Swelling of Female Pudendum; 前庭大腺囊肿; 前庭大腺炎; 外阴血肿; 外阴炎; 阴户肿痛
V021-N00236.definitions: painful swelling of the female external genitalia

V021-N00237.label: 难产
V021-N00237.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00238.label: 鬼胎
V021-N00238.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00238.aliases: 鬼胎; 葡萄胎; 侵蚀性葡萄胎; 伪胎
V021-N00238.definitions: abnormal pregnancy resulting in a mass in the uterus resembling a bunch of grapes, referring to hydatidiform mole

V021-N00239.label: 黄带
V021-N00239.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00239.definitions: yellowish viscous discharge from the vagina, usually indicating the presence of pathogenic damp-heat

V021-N00240.label: 眼科
V021-N00240.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00241.label: 上胞下垂
V021-N00241.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00241.aliases: 上胞下垂; BYB090; 睢目; 睑废; 睑皮垂缓; 胞垂; 侵风; 上睑下垂; 眼睑垂缓
V021-N00241.englishNames: blepharoptosis; Blepharoptosis; Upper Eyelid Ptosis; drooping of upper eyelid(blepharoptosis); ptosis of eyelid
V021-N00241.definitions: drooping of the upper eyelid, impairing the vision if it covers the pupil

V021-N00242.label: 云雾移睛
V021-N00242.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00244.label: 冷泪

V021-N00244.englishNames: cold tearing; Cold Tear; Epiphora; cold tear; epiphora
V021-N00244.definitions: dacryorrhea of cold type, with no redness, pain or opacity of the eye

V021-N00245.label: 凝脂翳
V021-N00245.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00245.aliases: 凝脂翳; BYH030; 细菌性角膜炎
V021-N00245.englishNames: purulent keratitis; congealed-fat nebula (purulent keratitis); coagulated fatty nebula; ser pent comeal ulcer and comeal ulcer in fected by pseudomonas
V021-N00245.definitions: severe keratitis with purulent disintegration of the cornea

V021-N00246.label: 口眼喎斜

V021-N00247.label: 圆翳内障
V021-N00247.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00247.aliases: 圆翳内障; BYT040; 如银内障; 沉翳; 浮翳; 老年性白内障; 园翳内障; LENS OPACITY INTER OCULOPATHY

V021-N00249.label: 异物入目
V021-N00249.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00250.label: 惊震内障
V021-N00250.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00251.label: 撞击伤目

V021-N00257.label: 火疳

V021-N00258.label: 热泪

V021-N00260.label: 白睛溢血

V021-N00263.label: 目暗
V021-N00263.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00264.label: 目盲
V021-N00264.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00266.label: 眵
V021-N00266.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00266.definitions: secretion of the eye, thin or mucilaginous or even pus-like

V021-N00267.label: 睑废
V021-N00267.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00268.label: 睑弦赤烂
V021-N00268.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00268.aliases: 睑弦赤烂; BYB050; 风弦赤烂; Blepharitis Marginalis; Eyelid Marginal Ulceration

V021-N00270.aliases: 瞳神干缺; BYT011; 瞳人干缺

V021-N00274.label: 羞明
V021-N00274.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00275.label: 翳
V021-N00275.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00279.aliases: 胞生痰核; BYB020; 胞睑痰核; 睑板腺囊肿; 睥生痰核; 霰粒肿; 疣病

V021-N00290.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00295.label: 耳鼻喉口齿科
V021-N00295.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00296.label: 乳蛾
V021-N00296.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00296.aliases: 乳蛾; 喉蛾; Acute Tonsillitis; Tonsillitis

V021-N00298.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00299.label: 唇疔

V021-N00299.definitions: furuncle on the lip or at the corner of the mouth, small but deep-rooted, with pustule formation

V021-N00300.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00301.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00303.label: 喉痹

V021-N00304.label: 喉瘤

V021-N00626.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00626.englishNames: cardio-diaphragmatic region; cardiodiaphragmatic interspace; Inter Cardiodiaphragmatic Part; gao huang; inter cardiodiaphragmatic part
V021-N00626.definitions: the space inferior to the heart and superior to the diaphragm

V021-N00627.label: 膻中
V021-N00627.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00627.definitions: the center of the chest between the nipples

V021-N00628.label: 苗窍
V021-N00628.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00628.definitions: the sense organs that reflect the change of qi, blood, yin and yang, also known as signaling orifices/sense organs

V021-N00629.label: 营分
V021-N00629.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00629.definitions: that stratum of the body between the qi and blood aspects

V021-N00630.label: 蒂丁
V021-N00630.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00630.definitions: the pendular fleshy lobe in the middle of the posterior border of the soft palate, usually referring to uvula palatina

V021-N00631.label: 血分
V021-N00631.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00631.definitions: the deepest stratum of the body involved in the severest stage of an acute febrile disease

V021-N00632.label: 血轮
V021-N00632.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00632.definitions: the canthus, also the same as blood orbiculus

V021-N00633.label: 赤白肉际
V021-N00633.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00633.englishNames: border between the red and white flesh
V021-N00633.definitions: the skin boundary between the palm or sole (red in color) and the back of the hand or foot (white in color), respectively

V021-N00634.label: 辅骨
V021-N00634.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00634.definitions: the bony prominences on the sides of the knee, namely, the condyles of femur and the condyles of tibia

V021-N00635.label: 锐眦
V021-N00635.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00635.definitions: the canthus closer to the temple, the same as lesser canthus

V021-N00636.label: 頞
V021-N00636.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00636.definitions: that part of the nose formed by the junction of its lateral surfaces, the same as dorsum nasi

V021-N00637.label: 颃颡
V021-N00637.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00637.definitions: the upper part of the pharynx continuous with the nasal passages

V021-N00638.label: 颈骨
V021-N00638.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00638.definitions: a collective term for the cervical vertebrae

V021-N00639.label: 风轮
V021-N00639.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00639.definitions: the cornea, also the same as wind orbiculus

V021-N00640.label: 骸
V021-N00640.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00640.definitions: the supportive structure or framework of the body

V021-N00641.label: 高骨
V021-N00641.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00641.definitions: any bony process of the body surface, particularly referring to the styloid process of the radius

V021-N00642.label: 黑睛
V021-N00642.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00642.definitions: the transparent membranous structure forming the central anterior part of the eye, i.e. the cornea

V021-N00643.label: 鼻准
V021-N00643.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00643.definitions: the most distal portion of the nose, the same as apex nasi

V021-N00644.label: 病因类
V021-N00644.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00645.label: 七情
V021-N00645.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00645.definitions: a collective term for joy, anger, thought, anxiety, sorrow, fear and fright, taken as endogenous factors causing diseases if in excess

V021-N00646.label: 三因
V021-N00646.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00646.englishNames: three categories of disease cause; three causes
V021-N00646.definitions: external cause, internal cause, and cause neither internal nor external － an ancient classification of causes of disease, also called three categories of causes of disease

V021-N00647.label: 不內外因
V021-N00647.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00647.definitions: one of the three causes of disease, including dietary irregularities, overexertion and fatigue, traumatic injuries, and insect and animal bites

V021-N00648.label: 五味偏嗜
V021-N00648.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00648.definitions: habitual preference for a particular flavor or taste that may give rise to disease, the same as flavor craving/ preference

V021-N00649.label: 五志
V021-N00649.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00649.definitions: a collective term for joy, anger, thought, anxiety, and fear, the same as five emotions

V021-N00650.label: 五志化火
V021-N00650.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00650.englishNames: five emotions transforming into fire; transformation of the five minds into fire; five minds transforming into fire
V021-N00650.definitions: transformation of the five emotions (anger, joy, grief, thought and fear) into heat with fire symptoms, the same as transformation of the five emotions into fire

V021-N00651.label: 五志过极
V021-N00651.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00651.definitions: excessive anger, joy, grief, thought and fear, which may disturb the normal flow of qi and blood of the internal organs, the same as five excessive emotions

V021-N00652.label: 伏气
V021-N00652.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00652.definitions: the pathogen that induces the onset of disease after a latent period, also called incubative pathogen

V021-N00653.label: 六淫
V021-N00653.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00653.definitions: a collective term for the six excessive or untimely climatic influences as external pathogenic factors: wind, cold, summerheat, dampness, dryness and fire, also the same as six climatic pathogenic factors

V021-N00654.label: 内伤
V021-N00654.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00654.englishNames: endogenous injury; internal damage; Internal Damage
V021-N00654.definitions: any detrimental effect on visceral qi produced by intemperance of the seven emotions, overexertion and fatigue, improper diet and sexual overindulgence

V021-N00655.label: 内因
V021-N00655.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00655.definitions: one of the three causes of disease, one that arises within the body, referring chiefly to the excessive emotional changes

V021-N00656.label: 内寒
V021-N00656.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00656.definitions: cold in the interior due to deficiency of yang qi or preponderance of yin cold

V021-N00657.label: 内毒
V021-N00657.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00657.definitions: heat toxins dormant in the body

V021-N00658.label: 内燥
V021-N00658.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00658.definitions: dryness in the interior due to consumption of body fluid

V021-N00659.label: 内风
V021-N00659.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00659.aliases: 内风; 肝风内动; 虚风内动
V021-N00659.definitions: the same as liver wind, wind in the interior due to abnormal movement of body’s yang qi

V021-N00660.label: 凉燥
V021-N00660.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00660.definitions: the pathogenic factor that causes cool dryness pattern/syndrome

V021-N00661.label: 劳倦
V021-N00661.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00661.definitions: abnormal degree of fatigue brought about by excessive activity, the same as overstrain

V021-N00662.label: 合邪
V021-N00662.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00662.definitions: any combination of more than one pathogen

V021-N00663.label: 喜
V021-N00663.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00663.definitions: one of the seven emotions that in excess may make the heart qi sluggish, resulting in absentmindedness, palpitations, insomnia and even mental disturbance

V021-N00664.label: 外因
V021-N00664.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00664.definitions: one of the three causes of disease, one that originates outside the body, referring chiefly to the six excesses and pestilential pathogens

V021-N00665.label: 外寒
V021-N00665.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00665.definitions: cold as one of the six excesses that causes external cold pattern/syndrome

V021-N00666.label: 外感
V021-N00666.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00666.definitions: disease or morbid condition produced by any of the six excesses (external etiological factors) or other noxious factors, the same as exopathic disease

V021-N00667.label: 外湿
V021-N00667.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00667.definitions: dampness as one of the six excesses that causes externally contracted dampness pattern/syndrome

V021-N00668.label: 外燥
V021-N00668.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00668.definitions: dryness as one of the six excesses that causes external dryness pattern/syndrome

V021-N00669.label: 外邪
V021-N00669.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00669.definitions: any pathogen originating outside the body

V021-N00670.label: 外风
V021-N00670.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00670.definitions: wind as one of the six excesses that causes external wind pattern/syndrome

V021-N00671.label: 寒
V021-N00671.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00671.definitions: cold as a pathogenic factor characterized by the damage to yang qi, deceleration of activity, congealing and contracting actions, also called pathogenic cold

V021-N00672.label: 寒毒
V021-N00672.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00672.definitions: a cold pathogen with virulent coldness

V021-N00673.label: 寒湿
V021-N00673.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00673.definitions: a combined pathogen of cold and dampness

V021-N00674.label: 忧
V021-N00674.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00674.definitions: one of the seven emotions that in excess may cause damage to the lung, and in combination with thought may injure the spleen

V021-N00675.label: 怒
V021-N00675.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00675.definitions: one of the seven emotions that in excess may cause the liver qi to ascend together with blood, resulting in headache, flushed face, blood-shot eyes, or hematemesis, even sudden fainting

V021-N00676.label: 思
V021-N00676.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00676.definitions: one of the seven emotions that in excess may cause stagnation of the spleen qi, and even damage to the transporting and transforming function of the spleen

V021-N00677.label: 恐
V021-N00677.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00677.definitions: one of the seven emotions that in excess may cause the kidney qi to sink, resulting in incontinence of urine and stools, or even syncope

V021-N00678.label: 恶气
V021-N00678.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00678.definitions: (1) a general term for the pathogenic qi, including six excesses and pestilential factors; (2) a pathological product derived from stagnation of qi and blood; also called evil qi

V021-N00679.label: 悲
V021-N00679.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00679.definitions: one of the seven emotions that in excess may consume the lung qi, resulting in shortness of breath, listlessness and fatigue

V021-N00680.label: 惊
V021-N00680.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00680.definitions: one of the seven emotions that occurs suddenly, may disturb the heart qi, resulting in palpitations or mental confusion

V021-N00681.label: 房劳
V021-N00681.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00682.label: 时毒
V021-N00682.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00682.definitions: (1) virulent pathogens prevalent in a particular season or seasons; (2) painful swelling in the neck, cheek and jaw, caused by epidemic seasonal pathogens which invades the three yang meridians

V021-N00683.label: 时行戾气
V021-N00683.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00683.aliases: 时行戾气; 疠气; 疫毒
V021-N00683.definitions: pathogen that cause an epidemic infectious diseases, also called pestilential qi

V021-N00684.label: 时邪
V021-N00684.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00684.definitions: a general designation for the pathogenic factors causing seasonal diseases

V021-N00685.label: 暑
V021-N00685.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00685.definitions: summerheat as a pathogenic factor, only occurring in summer, characterized by hotness and evaporation, also called pathogenic summerheat

V021-N00686.label: 暑气
V021-N00686.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00687.label: 暑湿
V021-N00687.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00687.definitions: a combined pathogen of summerheat and dampness

V021-N00688.label: 暑热
V021-N00688.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00688.definitions: summerheat as a pathogen that gives rise to disease characterized by heat symptoms

V021-N00689.label: 毒
V021-N00689.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00689.definitions: any virulent pathogen that causes a fulminating disease

V021-N00690.label: 水土不服
V021-N00690.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00690.englishNames: failure to acclimatize to a new environment
V021-N00690.definitions: temporary inadaptability of a person to a new natural and living environment, the same as nonacclimatization

V021-N00691.label: 水湿
V021-N00691.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00691.definitions: any water or dampness as an entity to cause disease

V021-N00692.label: 浊邪
V021-N00692.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00692.definitions: pathogenic dampness-turbidity or phlegm-turbidity, usually impeding the movement of yang qi

V021-N00693.label: 温燥
V021-N00693.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00693.definitions: the pathogenic factor that causes warm dryness pattern/syndrome

V021-N00694.label: 温邪
V021-N00694.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00694.definitions: a collective term for various pathogens causing acute febrile diseases

V021-N00695.label: 湿
V021-N00695.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00695.definitions: dampness as a pathogenic factor characterized by its impediment to qi movement and its turbidity, heaviness, stickiness and downward flowing properties, also called pathogenic dampness

V021-N00696.label: 湿毒
V021-N00696.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00696.definitions: noxious pathogenic factor formed by stagnation of dampness, which may cause hematochezia when it occurs in the intestine, or ulcer of the shank when in the muscles and skin of the lower limbs

V021-N00697.label: 湿浊
V021-N00697.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00697.definitions: the same as dampness. It describes the heavy, viscid and turbid nature of dampness

V021-N00698.label: 湿火
V021-N00698.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00698.definitions: depressed dampness transforming into fire that damages spleen-stomach yin

V021-N00699.label: 湿热
V021-N00699.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00699.definitions: a combined pathogen of dampness and heat

V021-N00700.label: 火
V021-N00700.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00700.definitions: fire as a pathogenic factor characterized by intense heat that is apt to injure fluid, consume qi, engender wind, inducing bleeding, and disturb the mental activities, also called pathogenic fire

V021-N00701.label: 火毒
V021-N00701.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00701.definitions: (1) pathogenic agent formed in the course of smoldering of pathogenic fire; (2) burns or scalds complicated with infection

V021-N00702.label: 热
V021-N00702.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00702.definitions: heat as a pathogenic factor that causes heat pattern/ syndrome, also called pathogenic heat

V021-N00703.label: 热毒
V021-N00703.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00703.definitions: a virulent pathogen arising from accumulated pathogenic heat

V021-N00704.label: 燥
V021-N00704.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00704.definitions: dryness as a pathogenic factor characterized by dryness and is apt to injure the lung and consume fluid, also called pathogenic dryness

V021-N00705.label: 燥气
V021-N00705.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00706.label: 燥热
V021-N00706.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00706.definitions: a combined pathogen of dryness and heat

V021-N00707.label: 疫疠
V021-N00707.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00707.definitions: a collective term for various highly infectious epidemic diseases

V021-N00708.label: 病因
V021-N00708.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00709.label: 病因学说
V021-N00709.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00709.englishNames: disease cause theory; theory of causes of disease; etiology
V021-N00709.definitions: the theory dealing with the classification of pathogenic factors and their properties, and pathogenic characteristics and processes, the same as etiology

V021-N00710.label: 病邪
V021-N00710.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00710.definitions: an agent causing disease, also called pathogenic factor or pathogenic qi

V021-N00711.label: 痰
V021-N00711.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00711.definitions: (1) pathologic secretions of the diseased respiratory tract, which is known as sputum; (2) the viscous turbid pathological product that can accumulate in the body, causing a variety of diseases

V021-N00712.label: 痰湿
V021-N00712.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00712.definitions: a combination of phlegm and internal dampness as an entity to cause disease, the same as dampnessphlegm

V021-N00713.label: 痰饮
V021-N00713.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00713.definitions: a combination of phlegm and retained fluid as an entity to cause disease, also called phlegm-fluid retention

V021-N00714.label: 瘀血
V021-N00714.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00714.definitions: a pathological product of blood stagnation, including extravasated blood and the blood circulating sluggishly or blood congested in a viscus, all of which may turn into pathogenic factor, the same as blood stasis or stagnant blood

V021-N00715.label: 瘴毒
V021-N00715.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00715.definitions: noxious mountainous vapor alleged to be the cause of certain kinds of malaria, also known as miasma

V021-N00716.label: 禀赋不足
V021-N00716.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00716.definitions: congenital constitutional weakness as the main etiological factor of deficiency conditions

V021-N00717.label: 秽浊
V021-N00717.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00717.definitions: putrid and filthy qi that causes disease, including miasma

V021-N00718.label: 胎毒
V021-N00718.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00718.definitions: (1) toxic heat affecting the fetus; (2) any congenital pathogenic factor; also known as fetal toxicosis

V021-N00719.label: 蛊毒
V021-N00719.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00719.definitions: a pathogenic factor that may cause diseases marked by abdominal lumps, tympanites and ascites, also known as worm toxin

V021-N00720.label: 酒癖
V021-N00720.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00720.englishNames: liquor addiction; alcohol addiction; alcoholic abdominal mass
V021-N00720.definitions: the state of heavy dependence on alcohol

V021-N00721.label: 阳邪
V021-N00721.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00722.label: 阴邪
V021-N00722.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00723.label: 风
V021-N00723.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00723.definitions: wind as a pathogenic factor characterized by its rapid movement, swift changes, and ascending and opening actions, also called pathogenic wind

V021-N00724.label: 风寒
V021-N00724.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00724.definitions: a combined pathogen of external wind and cold

V021-N00725.label: 风寒湿
V021-N00725.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00725.definitions: a combined pathogen of wind, cold and dampness

V021-N00726.label: 风湿
V021-N00726.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00726.definitions: dampness produced in the body due to yang deficiency of the spleen and kidney with decreased fluid transportation and transformation and resultant water stagnation

V021-N00727.label: 风湿
V021-N00727.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00727.definitions: (1) a combined pathogen of external wind and dampness; (2) arthritis with soft tissue inflammation

V021-N00728.label: 风热
V021-N00728.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00728.definitions: a combined pathogen of external wind and heat

V021-N00729.label: 风燥
V021-N00729.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00729.definitions: a combined pathogen of external wind and dryness

V021-N00730.label: 风痰
V021-N00730.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00730.definitions: a combined pathogen of wind and phlegm

V021-N00731.label: 饮
V021-N00731.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00731.definitions: the clear and watery pathological product due to disordered fluid metabolism, also called fluid retention

V021-N00732.label: 饮食不节
V021-N00732.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00732.englishNames: irregular eating; dietary irregularities; Improper Diet
V021-N00732.definitions: diet harmful to health, including ingestion of raw, cold or contaminated food, voracious eating or excessive hunger, predilection for a special food, alcohol addiction, etc.

V021-N00733.label: 麻毒
V021-N00733.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00734.label: 病机类
V021-N00734.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00735.label: 上厥下竭
V021-N00735.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00735.englishNames: upper body reversal and lower body exhaustion
V021-N00735.definitions: a pathological change marked by exhaustion of genuine yin and yang in the lower part of the body with syncope

V021-N00736.label: 上寒下热
V021-N00736.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00736.englishNames: upper body cold and lower body heat
V021-N00736.definitions: a complex condition characterized by the simultaneous presence of cold in the upper body and heat in the lower body, the same as cold above and heat below

V021-N00737.label: 上热下寒
V021-N00737.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00737.englishNames: upper body heat and lower body cold
V021-N00737.definitions: a complex condition characterized by the simultaneous presence of heat in the upper body and cold in the lower body, the same as heat above and cold below

V021-N00738.label: 上盛下虚
V021-N00738.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00738.definitions: exuberance of pathogenic qi in the upper body and deficiency of healthy qi in the lower body, the same as excess above and deficiency below

V021-N00739.label: 上虚下实
V021-N00739.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00739.definitions: deficiency of healthy qi in the upper body and excess of pathogenic qi in the lower body, the same as deficiency above and excess below

V021-N00740.label: 下厥上冒
V021-N00740.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00740.englishNames: lower body reversal with upper body veiling
V021-N00740.definitions: a pathological change in which the spleen fails to send the clear up while the stomach qi rises to the head adversely, resulting in dizziness, blurred vision accompanied by nausea, vomiting, and epigastric distension and pain

V021-N00741.label: 下焦湿热
V021-N00741.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00741.definitions: a pathological change characterized by dampnessheat pouring down into the lower energizer, including large intestinal dampness-heat, bladder dampnessheat, dampness-heat obstructing the essence chamber, and morbid leukorrhea, pudental itch, and painful

V021-N00742.label: 不传
V021-N00742.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00742.definitions: no further development of febrile disease

V021-N00743.label: 中寒
V021-N00743.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00743.definitions: attack of cold directly to the stomach and intestines

V021-N00744.label: 中寒
V021-N00744.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00744.definitions: (1) middle energizer deficiency-cold arising from insufficiency of yang qi; (2) internal cold remaining in the middle energizer causing damage to yang qi and stagnation of qi and blood

V021-N00745.label: 中气下降
V021-N00745.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00745.definitions: a pathological change of deficient spleen qi marked by its failure in upbearing the clear and other lifting or holding functions

V021-N00746.label: 中气不足
V021-N00746.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00746.definitions: deficiency of qi in the middle energizer, marked by diminished function of the spleen and stomach

V021-N00747.label: 中湿
V021-N00747.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00747.definitions: (1) a pathological change attributed to dampness attack, either from without or from within; (2) a type of apoplexy related to contraction of dampness, the same as dampness attack

V021-N00748.label: 中阳不振
V021-N00748.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00748.definitions: weakness of yang qi in the middle energizer, marked by diminished function of the spleen and stomach in digestion and absorption

V021-N00749.label: 中风
V021-N00749.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00750.label: 亡血
V021-N00750.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00750.definitions: acute critical deficiency of blood, mostly due to massive hemorrhage, also called blood desertion

V021-N00751.label: 亡阳
V021-N00751.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00751.definitions: a pathological change where yang qi is suddenly exhausted, resulting in abrupt failure of bodily functions

V021-N00752.label: 亡阴
V021-N00752.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00752.definitions: a pathological change caused by sudden massive loss of fluid leading to collapse

V021-N00753.label: 从化
V021-N00753.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00753.definitions: the process of developing a pattern/syndrome in conformity with the patient’s constitution, e.g., heat pattern/syndrome in a patient of yang constitution, and cold pattern/syndrome in a patient of yin constitution

V021-N00754.label: 传化
V021-N00754.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00754.definitions: development of disease: transmission from one meridian/channel to another with shift of pathological changes

V021-N00755.label: 传变
V021-N00755.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00755.definitions: development of a disease: transmission from one meridian/channel to another with change in the manifestations

V021-N00756.label: 伤津
V021-N00756.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00756.definitions: a general term for various impairments and loss of body fluid

V021-N00757.label: 伤阳
V021-N00757.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00757.definitions: a general term for various kinds of pathological changes marked by impairment of yang qi

V021-N00758.label: 伤阴
V021-N00758.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00758.definitions: a general term for various kinds of pathological changes marked by consumption of yin qi

V021-N00759.label: 六郁
V021-N00759.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00759.definitions: a collective term for stagnation of qi, blood, damp, fire, phlegm and food, the same as six stagnations

V021-N00760.label: 内闭外脱
V021-N00760.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00760.definitions: a pathological change characterized by depression/ stagnation of excessive pathogenic qi in the interior with expulsion of the debilitated original qi

V021-N00761.label: 再经
V021-N00761.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00761.definitions: the process of a cold-damage disease in which one meridian/channel syndrome passes into another meridian/channel syndrome while the former persists

V021-N00762.label: 冲任不固
V021-N00762.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00762.englishNames: insecurity of the thoroughfare and conception vessels
V021-N00762.definitions: loss of function to control menstruation and prevent uterine bleeding and abortion by the thoroughfare and conception vessels

V021-N00763.label: 冲任不调
V021-N00763.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00763.englishNames: disharmony of the thoroughfare and conception vessels
V021-N00763.definitions: impeded flow of qi and blood in the thoroughfare and conception vessels causing dysfunction of these vessels

V021-N00764.label: 冲任损伤
V021-N00764.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00764.englishNames: damage to the thoroughfare and conception vessels
V021-N00764.definitions: a pathological change arising when sexual overindulgence, frequent pregnancy or external pathogens cause damage to the thoroughfare and conception vessels

V021-N00765.label: 劳复
V021-N00765.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00765.definitions: relapse of disease due to over-fatigue

V021-N00766.label: 化寒
V021-N00766.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00766.definitions: transformation into a cold pattern/syndrome in the course of disease

V021-N00767.label: 化湿
V021-N00767.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00767.definitions: transformation into a dampness pattern/syndrome in the course of disease

V021-N00768.label: 化火
V021-N00768.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00768.definitions: transformation into a fire pattern/syndrome in the course of disease

V021-N00769.label: 化热
V021-N00769.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00769.definitions: transformation into a heat pattern/syndrome in the course of disease

V021-N00770.label: 化燥
V021-N00770.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00770.definitions: transformation into a dryness pattern/syndrome due to consumption of body fluids

V021-N00771.label: 化风
V021-N00771.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00771.definitions: transformation into a wind pattern/syndrome in the course of disease

V021-N00772.label: 半表半里
V021-N00772.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00772.definitions: location between the exterior and the interior

V021-N00773.label: 卒发
V021-N00773.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00773.definitions: the rapid appearance of symptoms after contraction

V021-N00774.label: 卫弱营强
V021-N00774.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00774.definitions: the pathological state of spontaneous sweating without fever in an exterior pattern/syndrome

V021-N00775.label: 卫强营弱
V021-N00775.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00775.definitions: the pathological state of sweating that occurs only during fever in an exterior pattern/syndrome

V021-N00776.label: 卫气同病
V021-N00776.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00776.englishNames: disease of both defense and qi aspects
V021-N00776.definitions: coexistence of diseases in both the defense and qi aspects

V021-N00777.label: 卫营同病
V021-N00777.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00777.englishNames: disease of both defense and nutrient aspects
V021-N00777.definitions: coexistence of diseases in both the defense and nutrient aspects

V021-N00778.label: 卫阳被遏
V021-N00778.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00778.definitions: a pathological change characterized by the depressed defense qi which fails to warm and protect the body surface

V021-N00779.label: 合病
V021-N00779.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00779.definitions: combination of two or more meridian/channel diseases appearing at the same time

V021-N00780.label: 命门火衰
V021-N00780.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00780.englishNames: debilitation of the life gate fire
V021-N00780.definitions: a pathological change characterized by impaired reproductive function due to insufficiency of kidney yang

V021-N00781.label: 大肠实
V021-N00781.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00781.definitions: excess of pathogens in the large intestine

V021-N00782.label: 大肠实热
V021-N00782.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00782.definitions: a pathological change characterized by exuberant pathogenic heat obstructing the bowel qi

V021-N00783.label: 大肠寒结
V021-N00783.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00783.definitions: a pathological change characterized by retention of cold in the large intestine with constipation, the same as large intestinal cold accumulation

V021-N00784.label: 大肠液亏
V021-N00784.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00784.englishNames: insufficiency of the large intestinal humor
V021-N00784.definitions: a pathological change ascribed to insufficient fluid in the large intestine

V021-N00785.label: 大肠湿热
V021-N00785.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00785.definitions: a pathological change characterized by accumulation of dampness-heat in the large intestine which impairs the qi movement, damages the intestinal blood vessels, and causes abnormal bowel evacuations

V021-N00786.label: 大肠热
V021-N00786.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00786.definitions: excess of pathogenic heat in the large intestine

V021-N00787.label: 大肠热结
V021-N00787.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00787.definitions: a pathological change characterized by exuberant heat in the large intestine that causes dryness and constipation, the same as large intestinal heat accumulation

V021-N00788.label: 大肠虚
V021-N00788.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00788.definitions: deficiency of the large intestinal qi with abnormal bowel evacuations

V021-N00789.label: 大肠虚寒
V021-N00789.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00789.definitions: a pathological change in which debilitation of yang qi in the large intestine leads to endogenous cold and causes abnormal bowel evacuations

V021-N00790.label: 女劳复
V021-N00790.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00790.definitions: relapse of disease due to intemperance in sexual activity

V021-N00791.label: 实
V021-N00791.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00791.definitions: (1) excessiveness of the pathogenic qi; (2) strong constitution; (3) strong reaction against pathogens

V021-N00792.label: 实中夹虚
V021-N00792.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00792.definitions: excess condition complicated by deficiency symptoms with the former dominant

V021-N00793.label: 实寒
V021-N00793.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00793.definitions: a pathological change arising from contraction of yin cold pathogen

V021-N00794.label: 寒入血室
V021-N00794.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00794.definitions: the pathogenic cold entering the uterus, congeals the liver meridian and impedes blood flow

V021-N00795.label: 寒凝气滞
V021-N00795.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00795.englishNames: qi stagnation due to cold congealing
V021-N00795.definitions: congealing effect of pathogenic cold that leads to stagnant movement of qi

V021-N00796.label: 寒包火
V021-N00796.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00796.definitions: a pathological change characterized by contraction of wind-cold with accumulated internal heat

V021-N00797.label: 寒化
V021-N00797.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00797.definitions: the process of forming a cold pattern/syndrome in the course of disease

V021-N00798.label: 寒湿困脾
V021-N00798.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00798.definitions: a pathological change in which excessive colddampness impairs spleen yang, the same as colddampness accumulating in the spleen

V021-N00799.label: 寒滞肝脉
V021-N00799.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00799.englishNames: cold stagnating in the liver vessel
V021-N00799.definitions: a pathological change marked by cold pathogen congealing in the liver meridian

V021-N00800.label: 寒热
V021-N00800.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00800.definitions: (1) a pair of principles for differentiating the nature of disease: when yin is exuberant, there is cold; when yang prevails, there is heat; (2) chills and fever

V021-N00801.label: 寒热格拒
V021-N00801.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00801.definitions: a form of pathological change in which extremely excessive cold forces heat to the extremities, and vice versa

V021-N00802.label: 寒热错杂
V021-N00802.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00802.definitions: a pathological change that causes complicated heat and cold conditions such as heat in the upper body with cold in the lower body, and cold in the exterior and heat in the interior

V021-N00803.label: 小肠实热
V021-N00803.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00803.definitions: a pathological change of excess heat ascribed to shifting of heart fire to the small intestine

V021-N00804.label: 小肠虚寒
V021-N00804.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00804.definitions: impairment of the small intestine by insufficient yang qi with endogenous cold, affecting the separation of the clear and turbid

V021-N00805.label: 少阴寒化
V021-N00805.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00805.definitions: of heart-kidney yang with exuberance of yin cold, and usually manifested by aversion to cold with no fever, listlessness and sleepiness, reversal cold of the limbs, lienteric diarrhea and faint fine pulse

V021-N00806.label: 少阴热化
V021-N00806.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00806.definitions: a pathological change characterized by depletion of heart-kidney yin with exuberance of yang heat, and usually manifested by fever, vexation, insomnia, dry mouth and throat, reddened tongue and rapid fine pulse

V021-N00807.label: 并病
V021-N00807.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00807.definitions: overlapping of two meridian/channel diseases, in which they appear in succession and then coexist

V021-N00808.label: 徐发
V021-N00808.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00808.definitions: the gradual appearance of symptoms after contraction

V021-N00809.label: 循经传
V021-N00809.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00809.definitions: transmission of a cold-damage disease from one meridian/channel to another, by the order of greater yang, yang brightness, lesser yang, greater yin, lesser yin and reverting yin

V021-N00810.label: 心气不宁
V021-N00810.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00810.definitions: a pathological change characterized by a feeling of uneasiness together with severe palpitations, susceptibility to fright, vexation and insomnia, the same as restless heart qi

V021-N00811.label: 心气不收
V021-N00811.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00811.definitions: a pathological condition in which the heart qi floats astray, characterized by dissipated spirit, heart palpitations, and susceptibility to fright; the same as insecurity/dispersion of heart qi

V021-N00812.label: 心气盛
V021-N00812.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00812.definitions: a pathological change in which pathogenic factors make the heart qi exuberant, resulting in anxiety or other mental abnormalities

V021-N00813.label: 心气虚
V021-N00813.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00813.definitions: a pathological state showing hypofunction of the heart qi, usually manifested by palpitations, shortness of breath, oppressed feelings in the precordial region, spontaneous sweating and weak irregular pulse

V021-N00814.label: 心火上炎
V021-N00814.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00814.definitions: a pathological change in which fire flares upward along the heart meridian, causing mental restlessness and oral or lingual erosion

V021-N00815.label: 心火亢盛
V021-N00815.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00815.definitions: a pathological change marked by exuberant fire of the heart that causes mental disturbances, damage to fluid, and bleeding

V021-N00816.label: 心火内炽
V021-N00816.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00816.definitions: a pathological change marked by exuberant heart fire that disturbs mental activities, causing vexation, insomnia, throbbing palpitation, restlessness, or even mania

V021-N00817.label: 心肝火旺
V021-N00817.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00817.definitions: a pathological change in which exuberant liver fire and heart fire disturb mental activity, or cause vascular damage and frenetic blood flow, the same as hyperactive heart-liver fire

V021-N00818.label: 心肝血虚
V021-N00818.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00818.definitions: a pathological change in which deficiency of heart blood and liver blood deprives the mental activities, head, eyes, sinews and nails of nourishment

V021-N00819.label: 心肺气虚
V021-N00819.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00819.definitions: a pathological change characterized by deficiency of both heart qi and lung qi with impairment of blood circulation and qi diffusion

V021-N00820.label: 心肾不交
V021-N00820.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00820.englishNames: non-interaction between the heart and kidney
V021-N00820.definitions: a disorder of the normal relationship between the heart and kidney ascribed to deficiency of kidney yin and stirring of heart fire

V021-N00821.label: 心胃火燔
V021-N00821.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00821.definitions: a pathological change arising when intense heart heat gives rise to mental disquiet and stomach fire causes damage to fluids

V021-N00822.label: 心脾两虚
V021-N00822.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00822.definitions: a pathological change in which deficiency of both heart blood and spleen qi leads to disordered heart function and disquietness together with failure of the spleen in transportation and transformation

V021-N00823.label: 心营过耗
V021-N00823.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00823.definitions: a pathological change in which the nutrient of the heart is excessively consumed by heat or a chronic deficiency conditions, causing emaciation, night fever, and vexation

V021-N00824.label: 心虚胆怯
V021-N00824.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00824.definitions: a pathological change characterized by simultaneous deficiency of heart qi and gallbladder qi, marked by disquietness and susceptibility to fright and fear

V021-N00825.label: 心血瘀阻
V021-N00825.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00825.definitions: a pathological change of the heart in which the blood flow in the heart vessels is impeded, causing a feeling of suffocation and precordial pain

V021-N00826.label: 心血虚
V021-N00826.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00826.definitions: a pathological change of the heart that causes dizziness, insomnia, dream-disturbed sleep, palpitation and thready weak pulse

V021-N00827.label: 心阳虚
V021-N00827.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00827.definitions: a pathological change referring to diminution of the heart function in controlling blood and vessels and

V021-N00828.label: 心阴虚
V021-N00828.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00828.definitions: a pathological change of the heart in which deficiency of yin fails to check yang and results in relative preponderance of heart yang with such manifestations as mental unsteadiness, insomnia, night sweats, and feverish sensation in the palms of the h

V021-N00829.label: 戴阳
V021-N00829.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00829.definitions: a pathological state characterized by exuberant yin cold in the lower part of the body which forces the asthenic yang to stay at the upper surface of the body

V021-N00830.label: 暑入阳明
V021-N00830.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00830.definitions: penetration of summerheat into yang brightness, giving rise to intense heat involving the body surface, the heart and the head, and forcing the discharge of sweat, manifested by high fever, thirst, vexation, headache, dizziness, and sweating

V021-N00831.label: 正邪相争
V021-N00831.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00831.englishNames: struggle between the healthy qi and pathogenic qi
V021-N00831.definitions: the basic mechanism of disease in which any disease is considered as the process of struggle between the healthy qi and pathogenic qi

V021-N00832.label: 气不化津
V021-N00832.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00832.definitions: insufficient yang qi with reduced vaporizing action failing to induce the formation of body fluid

V021-N00833.label: 气分寒
V021-N00833.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00833.definitions: a pathological change characterized by cold in the qi aspect, either due to spleen-stomach yang deficiency with preponderance of yin cold or due to cold pathogen invading the qi aspect

V021-N00834.label: 气分热
V021-N00834.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00834.definitions: a pathological change characterized by heat in the qi aspect, mostly excess in nature

V021-N00835.label: 气化不利
V021-N00835.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00835.definitions: a pathological change of yang qi deficiency that decreases digestion and absorption, or impairs fluid metabolism leading to water-dampness retention or phlegm retention

V021-N00836.label: 气机不利
V021-N00836.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
