V021-N00836.definitions: impeded, obstructed or stagnant qi movement that impairs the functions of viscera and meridians/ channels, the same as qi movement depression

V021-N00837.label: 气机失调
V021-N00837.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00837.definitions: a general term for disordered activity of qi in ascending, descending, exiting and entering

V021-N00838.label: 气机郁滞
V021-N00838.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00838.definitions: depressed and stagnant flow of qi that causes dysfunction of internal organs and meridians/ channels, the same as qi stagnation

V021-N00839.label: 气滞
V021-N00839.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00839.definitions: a pathological change characterized by impeded circulation of qi that leads to stagnation of qi movement and functional disorder of organs, manifested as distention or pain in the affected part

V021-N00840.label: 气滞血瘀
V021-N00840.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00840.englishNames: blood stasis due to qi stagnation
V021-N00840.definitions: a pathological change in which a long-standing or severe stagnation of qi impedes the flow of blood, a condition characterized by coexistence of qi stagnation and blood stasis

V021-N00841.label: 气病及血
V021-N00841.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00841.definitions: a pathological change in which diseased conditions of qi tend to induce blood disorders

V021-N00842.label: 气脱
V021-N00842.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00842.definitions: desertion of qi due to sudden damage to the healthy qi, massive loss of blood, profuse sweating, excessive vomiting and diarrhea, or due to prolonged consumption in a chronic disease

V021-N00843.label: 气营两燔
V021-N00843.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00843.englishNames: blazing of both qi and nutrient
V021-N00843.definitions: a pathological change characterized by simultaneous existence of exuberant pathogenic heat in the qi and nutrient aspects

V021-N00844.label: 气虚
V021-N00844.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00844.definitions: a general term for deficiency of qi that leads to decreased visceral functions and lowered body resistance

V021-N00845.label: 气虚不摄
V021-N00845.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00845.englishNames: qi deficiency failing to control blood
V021-N00845.definitions: a pathological change of qi deficiency in which qi is unable to retain the fluids (including blood)

V021-N00846.label: 气虚中满
V021-N00846.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00846.englishNames: qi deficiency with fullness in the middle
V021-N00846.definitions: a pathological change of qi deficiency in which qi is insufficient for normal transportation and transformation in the middle energizer, thus causing epigastric and abdominal distension

V021-N00847.label: 气虚血瘀
V021-N00847.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00847.definitions: a pathological change of qi deficiency in which qi is insufficient to maintain blood flow, thus resulting in blood stasis

V021-N00848.label: 气血两燔
V021-N00848.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00848.englishNames: blazing of both qi and blood
V021-N00848.definitions: a pathological change characterized by the presence of exuberant pathogenic heat in both the qi and blood aspects causing damage to body fluids and hemorrhages

V021-N00849.label: 气血两虚
V021-N00849.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00849.englishNames: dual deficiency of qi and blood
V021-N00849.definitions: simultaneous presence of qi deficiency and blood deficiency

V021-N00850.label: 气血失调
V021-N00850.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00850.definitions: any failure in the mutually coordinating relationship of qi and blood

V021-N00851.label: 气逆
V021-N00851.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00851.definitions: reversal of the normal downward flow of qi, the same as qi reflux

V021-N00852.label: 气郁化火
V021-N00852.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00852.definitions: a pathological change of long-standing stagnation of qi that transforms into fire

V021-N00853.label: 气闭
V021-N00853.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00853.definitions: a pathological change marked by obstruction of the normal movement of qi

V021-N00854.label: 气阴两虚
V021-N00854.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00854.englishNames: dual deficiency of qi and yin
V021-N00854.definitions: simultaneous presence of qi deficiency and yin deficiency

V021-N00855.label: 气陷
V021-N00855.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00855.definitions: a pathological change of deficient qi marked by failure in its lifting or holding function, also known as qi sinking

V021-N00856.label: 气随液脱
V021-N00856.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00856.englishNames: qi collapse due to humor depletion
V021-N00856.definitions: a pathological change in which qi collapse occurs following massive loss of fluid

V021-N00857.label: 气随血脱
V021-N00857.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00857.definitions: a pathological change in which massive loss of blood leads to collapse of qi

V021-N00858.label: 水停气阻
V021-N00858.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00858.englishNames: water retention due to obstruction of qi
V021-N00858.definitions: a pathological change in which retention of water in the body causes obstruction of qi movement

V021-N00859.label: 水气凌心
V021-N00859.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00859.definitions: a pathological change in which upsurge of retained water causes disturbances of the heart

V021-N00860.label: 津亏血瘀
V021-N00860.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00860.definitions: a pathological change in which fluid deficiency causes stagnation of blood flow

V021-N00861.label: 津枯血燥
V021-N00861.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00861.definitions: a pathological change in which insufficiency of fluid gives rise to blood dryness with endogenous heat

V021-N00862.label: 津液亏损
V021-N00862.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00862.definitions: a pathological change marked by insufficiency of body fluid-humor that fails to moisten the body tissues

V021-N00863.label: 津脱
V021-N00863.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00863.definitions: a severe form of damage to fluid, usually due to profuse sweating, excessive vomiting or diarrhea

V021-N00864.label: 湿伤脾阳
V021-N00864.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00864.definitions: a pathological change characterized by stagnant cold-dampness causing damage to spleen yang

V021-N00865.label: 湿伤脾阴
V021-N00865.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00865.definitions: a pathological change in which the depressed dampness transforms into heat causing damage to spleen yin

V021-N00866.label: 湿化
V021-N00866.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00866.definitions: the process of forming a dampness pattern/syndrome in the course of disease

V021-N00867.label: 火化
V021-N00867.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00867.definitions: the process of forming a fire pattern/syndrome in the course of disease

V021-N00868.label: 火热迫肺
V021-N00868.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00868.definitions: a pathological change characterized by pathogenic fire-heat accumulating in the lung or intense fire-heat scorching lung yin

V021-N00869.label: 热伏冲任
V021-N00869.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00869.englishNames: hidden heat in the thoroughfare and conception vessels
V021-N00869.definitions: a morbid condition in which pathogenic heat stays in the thoroughfare and conception vessels

V021-N00870.label: 热伤神明
V021-N00870.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00870.definitions: mental disturbance such as coma, unconsciousness, delirium caused by high fever in febrile disease

V021-N00871.label: 热伤筋脉
V021-N00871.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00871.definitions: a pathological change characterized by intense heat scorching nutrient yin, depriving the sinews of nourishment and thereby causing contracture, flaccidity or paralysis of limbs

V021-N00872.label: 热入心包
V021-N00872.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00872.definitions: a morbid condition occurring in the nutrient and blood aspect of an epidemic febrile disease, manifested as high fever, coma, delirium, cold limbs or convulsion

V021-N00873.label: 热入血分
V021-N00873.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00873.definitions: a pathological change characterized by the pathogenic heat entering the blood aspect, causing hemorrhages, mental disturbances, and even convulsions

V021-N00874.label: 热入血室
V021-N00874.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00874.definitions: the pathogenic heat exploiting deficiency during menstruation or after childbirth to enter the blood chamber and contend with the blood

V021-N00875.label: 热化
V021-N00875.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00875.definitions: the process of forming a heat pattern/syndrome in the course of disease

V021-N00876.label: 热极生风
V021-N00876.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00876.definitions: a pathological change characterized by convulsions that arise when exuberant pathogenic heat scorches the liver meridian and deprives the sinews of nourishment

V021-N00877.label: 热灼肾阴
V021-N00877.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00877.definitions: damage to kidney yin by pathogenic heat, usually occurring in the advanced stage of warm heat disease, the same as heat damaging kidney yin

V021-N00878.label: 热盛伤津
V021-N00878.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00878.definitions: the process by which excess heat dissipates body fluids, also the same as excessive heat damaging fluid

V021-N00879.label: 热结
V021-N00879.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00879.definitions: a pathological change characterized by gathering and binding of pathogenic heat in the interior such as in the bladder, in the blood aspect, or in the gastrointestinal tract

V021-N00880.label: 热结下焦
V021-N00880.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00880.englishNames: heat binding in the lower energizer
V021-N00880.definitions: accumulation of heat in the intestines and bladder that causes lower abdominal distention and pain, constipation, short voidings of dark urine or even hematuria, and sometimes rigidity and tenderness of the lower abdomen accompanied by restlessness

V021-N00881.label: 热迫大肠
V021-N00881.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00881.definitions: a pathological change in which pathogenic heat impairs the function of the large intestine, leading to acute diarrhea with abdominal pain, burning sensation in the anus, scanty dark urine, and dry yellow tongue coating

V021-N00882.label: 热遏
V021-N00882.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00882.definitions: pathogenic heat is kept in the interior and cannot be discharged

V021-N00883.label: 热邪传里
V021-N00883.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00883.englishNames: pathogenic heat passing into the interior
V021-N00883.definitions: process of a disease where external heat pathogens enter the interior to cause an interior heat pattern/ syndrome

V021-N00884.label: 热郁
V021-N00884.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00884.definitions: a pathological change that arises when persistent depression transforms into heat, the same as heat stagnation

V021-N00885.label: 热闭
V021-N00885.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00885.definitions: pathogenic heat congests in the visceral organs and meridians/channels

V021-N00886.label: 燥化
V021-N00886.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00886.definitions: the process of forming a dryness pattern/syndrome due to fluid consumption

V021-N00887.label: 燥干清窍
V021-N00887.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00887.definitions: a pathological change characterized by dryness-heat affection of the upper part of the body impairing the function of sense organs

V021-N00888.label: 燥气伤肺
V021-N00888.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00888.definitions: a pathological change characterized by damage of the lung fluid by pathogenic dryness that impairs the diffusion and depurative downbearing function

V021-N00889.label: 燥结
V021-N00889.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00889.definitions: a pathological change characterized by lack of fluid in the gastrointestinal tract resulting in constipation

V021-N00890.label: 由实转虚
V021-N00890.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00890.definitions: the process of a disease turning from excess of pathogenic qi into deficiency of healthy qi

V021-N00891.label: 由虚转实
V021-N00891.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00891.definitions: the process of a disease turning from deficiency of healthy qi into excess of pathogenic qi

V021-N00892.label: 病位
V021-N00892.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00892.definitions: the part of the body affected by a disease

V021-N00893.label: 病性
V021-N00893.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00893.definitions: the heat, cold, excess or deficiency quality of a disease

V021-N00894.label: 病机
V021-N00894.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00894.definitions: the mechanism by which disease arises and develops, the same as pathogenesis

V021-N00895.label: 病机学说
V021-N00895.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00895.definitions: the theory that deals with the mechanism by which disease arises and develops

V021-N00896.label: 病证
V021-N00896.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00896.definitions: summarization of the cause, nature and location of the pathological change at a certain stages of disease

V021-N00897.label: 痰浊阻肺
V021-N00897.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00897.definitions: a pathological change characterized by impairment of diffusion and downbearing of lung qi due to phlegmdampness obstruction

V021-N00898.label: 痰火扰心
V021-N00898.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00898.definitions: a pathological change in which fire-heat and phlegmturbidity harassing the heart-spirit, lead to mental disturbance

V021-N00899.label: 痰蒙心包
V021-N00899.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00899.definitions: a pathological change in which phlegm causes mental confusion; the same as phlegm confounding the orifices of the heart

V021-N00900.label: 直中
V021-N00900.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00900.definitions: attack of an exogenous pathogen directly on the three yin meridians instead of transmission from the yang meridians, the same as direct attack

V021-N00901.label: 相火妄动
V021-N00901.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00901.englishNames: frenetic stirring of the ministerial fire
V021-N00901.definitions: deficiency of liver-kidney yin leading to hyperactivity and upsurge of ministerial fire

V021-N00902.label: 真实假虚
V021-N00902.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00903.label: 真寒假热
V021-N00903.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00903.definitions: a pathological change marked by abundant yin-cold in the interior with pseudo-heat manifestations

V021-N00904.label: 真热假寒
V021-N00904.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00904.definitions: a pathological change marked by excessive heat in the interior with pseudo-cold manifestations

V021-N00905.label: 真虚假实
V021-N00905.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00906.label: 神不守舍
V021-N00906.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00906.englishNames: spirit failing to keep to its abode
V021-N00906.definitions: mental derangement when the spirit is made to leave the heart

V021-N00907.label: 精脱
V021-N00907.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00907.definitions: a pathological change characterized by depletion and loss of kidney essence that leads to impaired hearing

V021-N00908.label: 经尽
V021-N00908.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00908.definitions: exogenous febrile disease stops progressing and begins recovering when it reaches a certain meridian/ channel or certain stage of development

V021-N00909.label: 经气逆乱
V021-N00909.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00909.definitions: a pathological change of meridians/channels in which the adverse flow of meridian/channel qi leads to disordered blood circulation

V021-N00910.label: 经隧失职
V021-N00910.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00910.definitions: a pathological change characterized by impaired flow of qi and blood in the meridians/channels

V021-N00911.label: 结阳
V021-N00911.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00911.definitions: sluggish flow of yang qi in the limbs, leading to retention of water and edema

V021-N00912.label: 结阴
V021-N00912.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00912.definitions: pathogenic qi binding in the yin meridians/channels.

V021-N00913.label: 肝实热
V021-N00913.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00913.definitions: a pathological state characterized by presence of exuberant fire-heat in the liver

V021-N00914.label: 肝寒
V021-N00914.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00914.definitions: (1) a morbid condition characterized by cold due to insufficient liver yang that causes congealing of liver qi with depression, timidity, lassitude, cold limbs, sunken thready pulse; (2) the same as cold stagnating in the liver vessel

V021-N00915.label: 肝气上逆
V021-N00915.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00915.definitions: the hyperactive liver qi attacking the upper part of the body, manifested as dizziness, headache, flushed face, tinnitus, deafness, fullness and pain of the chest and hypochondrium, belching, acid regurgitation, and even hematemesis, and a taut and fo

V021-N00916.label: 肝气不和
V021-N00916.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00916.definitions: a pathological change of the liver in its smoothing and discharging function, causing irritability, hypochondriac, mammary or lower abdominal distension and pain, and irregular menstruation

V021-N00917.label: 肝气不舒
V021-N00917.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00917.definitions: a disorder leading to functional disturbances of the liver, manifested as irritability, distension and pain of the chest, hypochondrium, and lower abdomen, distending pain of the breast and abnormal menstruation in the female

V021-N00918.label: 肝气实
V021-N00918.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00918.definitions: presence of excessive pathogenic qi in the liver or liver meridian; the same as exuberance of liver qi

V021-N00919.label: 肝气横逆
V021-N00919.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00919.definitions: a pathological change in which the depressed liver qi runs transversely, impairing the spleen and stomach

V021-N00920.label: 肝气犯胃
V021-N00920.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00920.definitions: a pathological change in which the depressed liver qi running transversely impairs the stomach function in food intake and digestion

V021-N00921.label: 肝气犯脾
V021-N00921.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00922.label: 肝气虚
V021-N00922.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00922.definitions: a morbid condition characterized by deficiency of qi resulting in impaired function of the liver in smoothing the flow of qi

V021-N00923.label: 肝气郁结
V021-N00923.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00923.definitions: stagnation of qi in the liver resulting from impairment of free coursing, also called liver depression

V021-N00924.label: 肝火
V021-N00924.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00924.definitions: a pathological change of exuberant liver qi with heat manifestations

V021-N00925.label: 肝火上炎
V021-N00925.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00925.definitions: a pathological change characterized by exuberant liver fire flaming upward to the head and eyes

V021-N00926.label: 肝火犯肺
V021-N00926.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00926.definitions: a pathological change characterized by exuberant liver fire ascending counterflow to invade the lung

V021-N00927.label: 肝热
V021-N00927.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00927.definitions: a general term for various heat syndromes of the liver such as liver fire and ascendant hyperactivity of liver yang

V021-N00928.label: 肝经实热
V021-N00928.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00928.englishNames: excess heat in the liver meridian
V021-N00928.definitions: accumulation of exuberant fire-heat in the liver meridian

V021-N00929.label: 肝经湿热
V021-N00929.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00929.definitions: a pathological change attributable to dampnessheat accumulated in the liver and pouring downward along the liver meridian

V021-N00930.label: 肝肾亏损
V021-N00930.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00930.definitions: a pathological change in which insufficient essence and blood of the liver and kidney fail to nourish the related body constituents and organs, but do not produce deficiency-fire

V021-N00931.label: 肝肾阴虚
V021-N00931.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00931.definitions: a pathological change in which insufficient yin fluid of the liver and kidney fails to nourish the related body constituents and organs, and gives rise to deficiencyfire symptoms

V021-N00932.label: 肝胆俱实
V021-N00932.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00932.definitions: accumulation of excessive pathogen (such as stagnant qi, impeded dampness, accumulated heat, static blood or calculi) in the liver and gallbladder

V021-N00933.label: 肝胆气郁
V021-N00933.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00933.definitions: stagnation of qi in the liver and gallbladder with impaired function of free coursing and bile discharge, the same as liver-gallbladder qi stagnation

V021-N00934.label: 肝胆湿热
V021-N00934.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00934.definitions: accumulation of dampness-heat in the liver and gallbladder resulting in impaired bile flow and downward pouring of dampness-heat

V021-N00935.label: 肝虚
V021-N00935.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00935.definitions: a general term for deficiency conditions of the liver, including deficiency of liver qi, liver blood, liver yin, and liver yang

V021-N00936.label: 肝血虚
V021-N00936.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00936.definitions: a morbid condition characterized by depletion of blood in the liver

V021-N00937.label: 肝郁脾虚
V021-N00937.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00937.definitions: a pathological change in which depressed liver qi impairs the transporting and transforming function of the spleen, the same as liver stagnation and spleen deficiency

V021-N00938.label: 肝阳上亢
V021-N00938.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N00939.label: 肝阳化火
V021-N00939.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00939.definitions: ascendant hyperactivity of liver yang giving rise to fire

V021-N00940.label: 肝阳化风
V021-N00940.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00940.definitions: hyperactivity of liver yang stirring the internal wind

V021-N00941.label: 肝阳虚
V021-N00941.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00941.definitions: a pathological change characterized by insufficient yang qi of the liver that reduces the function of free coursing and storing blood, and produces deficiency cold, also called liver deficiency cold

V021-N00942.label: 肝阴虚
V021-N00942.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00942.definitions: a pathological change characterized by insufficient yin fluid of the liver that fails to nourish the head, eyes, sinews and nails, and produces deficiency-heat

V021-N00943.label: 肝风
V021-N00943.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00943.aliases: 肝风内动; 风气内动
V021-N00943.definitions: abnormal movement of the body’s yang qi turning to internal wind, a general term for liver yang transforming into wind, extreme heat engendering wind, yin deficiency with stirring wind, and blood deficiency engendering wind, also called internal stir

V021-N00944.label: 肺失清肃
V021-N00944.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00944.definitions: a pathological change of lung that gives rise to cough, dyspnea, expectoration of sputum and fullness in the chest

V021-N00945.label: 肺实
V021-N00945.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00945.definitions: any excess condition of the lung, attributable to attack of the lung by external wind, cold, dryness, and heat pathogens or obstruction of the lung by phlegm-heat, phlegm-dampness, etc.

V021-N00946.label: 肺实热
V021-N00946.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00946.definitions: a pathological change characterized by presence of excessive pathogenic heat in the lung

V021-N00947.label: 肺寒
V021-N00947.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00947.definitions: a term either referring to external cold invading the lung or to deficiency-cold of lung qi

V021-N00948.label: 肺气上逆
V021-N00948.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00948.definitions: a pathological change characterized by counterflow ascending of lung qi instead of depurative downbearing

V021-N00949.label: 肺气不利
V021-N00949.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00949.definitions: any disturbance of depurative downbearing of lung qi and the lung’s governing of the waterways

V021-N00950.label: 肺气不宣
V021-N00950.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00950.definitions: disordered diffusion of lung qi generally attributable to external pathogens invading the lung or fettering the exterior, and manifesting in cough, hoarse voice, and nasal congestion, the same as lung qi failing to disperse

V021-N00951.label: 肺气虚
V021-N00951.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00951.definitions: a pathological change characterized by deficiency of qi with diminished function of the lung

V021-N00952.label: 肺津不布
V021-N00952.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00952.definitions: failure of the lung to distribute fluid, leading to production of phlegm and causing cough and dyspnea

V021-N00953.label: 肺火
V021-N00953.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00953.definitions: a pathological change characterized by presence of intense heat in the lung, either of excess type or of deficiency type

V021-N00954.label: 肺热
V021-N00954.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00954.definitions: a general term for heat conditions of the lung

V021-N00955.label: 肺络损伤
V021-N00955.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00955.definitions: a pathological change marked by expectoration of blood, caused by severe or enduring cough or by heat pathogen

V021-N00956.label: 肺肾气虚
V021-N00956.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00956.definitions: a pathological change characterized by dual deficiency of lung qi and kidney qi with impaired function of depurative downbearing and qi absorption

V021-N00957.label: 肺肾阴虚
V021-N00957.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00957.definitions: a pathological change characterized by deficiency of yin fluid of the lung and kidney with engendering of internal heat

V021-N00958.label: 肺脾两虚
V021-N00958.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00958.aliases: 脾肺两虚; 肺脾气虚
V021-N00958.definitions: qi deficiency of both the lung and spleen marked by failure of the spleen in transportation and transformation and the lung in diffusion and depurative downbearing; the same as lung-spleen qi deficiency

V021-N00959.label: 肺虚
V021-N00959.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00959.definitions: a general term for deficiency conditions of the lung, including lung qi deficiency and lung yin deficiency

V021-N00960.label: 肺阴虚
V021-N00960.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00960.definitions: a pathological change characterized by insufficient lung yin with endogenous heat

V021-N00961.label: 肾不纳气
V021-N00961.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00961.definitions: deficiency of the kidney qi preventing the reception of lung qi

V021-N00962.label: 肾实
V021-N00962.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00962.definitions: excessive pathogenic qi in the kidney

V021-N00963.label: 肾气不固
V021-N00963.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00963.definitions: a pathological change characterized by diminished essence-storing and astringing function of the kidney

V021-N00964.label: 肾气实
V021-N00964.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00964.definitions: repletion of the essential qi of the kidney

V021-N00965.label: 肾气虚
V021-N00965.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00965.definitions: a pathological change characterized by depletion of the kidney qi with diminished essence-storing and astringing function

V021-N00966.label: 肾火偏亢
V021-N00966.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00966.definitions: relative excess of kidney fire due to deficiency of kidney yin

V021-N00967.label: 肾热
V021-N00967.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00967.definitions: a pathological change characterized by kidney yin depletion with endogenous heat

V021-N00968.label: 肾精不足
V021-N00968.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00968.definitions: a pathological change of the kidney with insufficiency of its essence and impaired function

V021-N00969.label: 肾虚
V021-N00969.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00969.definitions: a general term for deficiency conditions of the kidney, including kidney yin deficiency, kidney yang deficiency, insufficiency of kidney essence, and insecurity of kidney qi

V021-N00970.label: 肾虚水泛
V021-N00970.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00970.definitions: a pathological change characterized by deficiency of kidney yang with impaired water metabolism and resultant retention of fluid

V021-N00971.label: 肾阳虚
V021-N00971.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00971.definitions: a pathological change characterized by deficiency of kidney yang with diminished warming function and failure in qi transformation

V021-N00972.label: 肾阴虚
V021-N00972.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00972.definitions: a pathological change characterized by deficiency of yin to nourish the kidney, leading to deficiency-fire or deficiency-heat

V021-N00973.label: 胃不和
V021-N00973.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00973.definitions: a general term referring to various functional disorders of the stomach, i.e., dysfunction in receiving and digesting food as well as in conducting the contents to the intestines

V021-N00974.label: 胃实
V021-N00974.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00974.definitions: a morbid condition due to consumption of body fluid and stagnation of stomach qi, caused by excessive heat accumulated in the stomach

V021-N00975.label: 胃家实
V021-N00975.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00975.definitions: a pathological change characterized by presence of pathogens deep in the yang brightness meridian that causes exuberant dryness-heat in the gastrointestinal tract

V021-N00976.label: 胃寒
V021-N00976.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00976.definitions: a pathological change either due to deficiency of stomach yang or caused by direct attack of pathogenic cold, the former being deficiency-cold of the stomach, and the latter, excess-cold in the stomach

V021-N00977.label: 胃气上逆
V021-N00977.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00977.definitions: a pathological change characterized by counterflow ascent of stomach qi that causes belching, hiccups, acid regurgitation and vomiting

V021-N00978.label: 胃气不降
V021-N00978.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00978.englishNames: stomach qi failing to bear downward
V021-N00978.definitions: impaired downbearing of stomach qi, causing anorexia, nausea, vomiting, belching, and a feeling of fullness in the epigastric region, the same as stomach qi failing to descend

V021-N00979.label: 胃气虚
V021-N00979.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00979.definitions: a pathological change characterized by weakness of stomach qi with impaired appetite and digestion

V021-N00980.label: 胃火上升
V021-N00980.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00980.definitions: a pathological change in which exuberant fire in the stomach flares up along the meridian/channel, causing toothache, gum swelling or bleeding, or hematemesis

V021-N00981.label: 胃火炽盛
V021-N00981.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00981.definitions: presence of excessive pathogenic heat in the stomach with upward flaming of stomach fire

V021-N00982.label: 胃热
V021-N00982.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00982.definitions: a pathological change due to impairment of the stomach by pathogenic heat or caused by overeating of hot pungent food

V021-N00983.label: 胃热消谷
V021-N00983.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00983.definitions: a pathological change in which stomach heat or fire leads to abnormally rapid digestion

V021-N00984.label: 胃虚
V021-N00984.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00984.definitions: a general term for deficiency conditions of the stomach，including qi deficiency, yin deficiency and yang deficiency of the stomach

V021-N00985.label: 胃阳虚
V021-N00985.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00985.definitions: a pathological change characterized by decline of yang qi which leads to deficiency-cold of the stomach with markedly impaired appetite and digestion

V021-N00986.label: 胃阴虚
V021-N00986.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00986.definitions: a pathological change characterized by deficiency of fluid in the stomach with impaired function attributable to intense stomach fire or exuberant heat in a warm disease

V021-N00987.label: 胆实热
V021-N00987.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00987.definitions: a pathological change characterized by excess heat in the gallbladder and gallbladder meridian

V021-N00988.label: 胆气不足
V021-N00988.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00988.definitions: a pathological change ascribed to insufficiency of the gallbladder qi that makes the mind disquieted and susceptible to fright

V021-N00989.label: 胆热
V021-N00989.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00989.definitions: a morbid condition ascribed to attack on the gallbladder and gallbladder meridian by pathogenic heat

V021-N00990.label: 脾不统血
V021-N00990.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00990.englishNames: spleen failing to control the blood
V021-N00990.definitions: a pathological change characterized by failure of the spleen to keep the blood flowing in the vessels, resulting in hemorrhage, the same as spleen failing to keep the blood within the vessel

V021-N00991.label: 脾失健运
V021-N00991.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00991.definitions: dysfunction of the spleen in transporting the essence of food and water

V021-N00992.label: 脾实
V021-N00992.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00992.definitions: a pathological state ascribed to accumulation of excessive pathogenic qi in the spleen, also the same as spleen qi excess

V021-N00993.label: 脾实热
V021-N00993.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00993.definitions: a pathological condition characterized by exuberant pathogenic heat in the spleen

V021-N00994.label: 脾寒
V021-N00994.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00994.definitions: a general term for various cold conditions of the spleen

V021-N00995.label: 脾气下陷
V021-N00995.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00995.definitions: a pathological change characterized as sinking of spleen qi induced by weakness of the spleen with diminished upbearing function

V021-N00996.label: 脾气不升
V021-N00996.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00996.englishNames: spleen qi failing to bear upward
V021-N00996.definitions: dysfunction of the spleen in upbearing nutrients, the same as spleen qi failing to ascend

V021-N00997.label: 脾气不舒
V021-N00997.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00997.definitions: a pathological change marked by dysfunction of the spleen with impaired digestion and absorption

V021-N00998.label: 脾气虚
V021-N00998.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00998.definitions: a pathological change characterized by qi deficiency with impaired transporting and transforming function of the spleen

V021-N00999.label: 脾热
V021-N00999.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N00999.definitions: a general term for various heat conditions of the spleen

V021-N01000.label: 脾肾阳虚
V021-N01000.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01000.definitions: a pathological change characterized by insufficient yang qi of the spleen and kidney with endogenous cold and flooding of water

V021-N01001.label: 脾胃俱实
V021-N01001.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01001.definitions: a pathological change marked by presence of excessive pathogenic qi in both the spleen and stomach

V021-N01002.label: 脾胃俱虚
V021-N01002.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01002.definitions: a deficiency condition of both the spleen and stomach, marked by a feeling of emptiness in the stomach, shortness of breath, reversal cold of the limbs and incessant watery diarrhea

V021-N01003.label: 脾胃湿热
V021-N01003.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01003.definitions: a pathological condition ascribed to accumulation of dampness-heat which impairs the functions of the spleen and stomach, the same as dampness-heat in the middle energizer

V021-N01004.label: 脾胃虚寒
V021-N01004.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01004.definitions: a pathological change characterized by decline of yang qi of the spleen and stomach with diminished functions and endogenous deficiency-cold

V021-N01005.label: 脾胃虚弱
V021-N01005.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01005.definitions: a pathological change characterized by diminished spleen and stomach function of food intake and digestion

V021-N01006.label: 脾胃阴虚
V021-N01006.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01006.definitions: a pathological change in which deficiency of yin fluid of the spleen-stomach results in impaired food intake and digestion

V021-N01007.label: 脾虚
V021-N01007.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01007.definitions: a general term for deficiency condition of the spleen, such as spleen qi deficiency, spleen yang deficiency, and spleen yin deficiency

V021-N01008.label: 脾虚湿困
V021-N01008.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01008.definitions: a complex condition of deficiency and excess wherein spleen deficiency leads to accumulation of dampnessturbidity, the same as spleen deficiency with dampness accumulation

V021-N01009.label: 脾虚生风
V021-N01009.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01009.definitions: a pathological change characterized by stirring of wind internally due to spleen deficiency

V021-N01010.label: 脾阳虚
V021-N01010.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01010.definitions: a pathological change characterized by deficiency of yang in the spleen resulting in deficiency-cold

V021-N01011.label: 脾阴虚
V021-N01011.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01011.definitions: a pathological change ascribed to deficiency of yin in the spleen with inadequate distribution of essence to the body

V021-N01012.label: 膀胱不利
V021-N01012.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01012.definitions: a pathological change of the bladder’s function in urination

V021-N01013.label: 膀胱失约
V021-N01013.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01013.definitions: impaired bladder function resulting in urinary incontinence

V021-N01014.label: 膀胱气闭
V021-N01014.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01014.definitions: impairment of bladder qi transformation resulting in inhibited and difficult urination or anuria

V021-N01015.label: 膀胱湿热
V021-N01015.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01015.definitions: accumulation of dampness-heat in the bladder

V021-N01016.label: 膀胱虚寒
V021-N01016.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01016.definitions: impaired activity of the urinary bladder with cold manifestations due to consumption of kidney yang

V021-N01017.label: 营卫不和
V021-N01017.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01017.definitions: the pathological state of spontaneous sweating in an exterior pattern/syndrome, as the defense aspect regulates the excretion of sweat while the nutrient aspect provides fluid for the formation of sweat

V021-N01018.label: 营阴郁滞
V021-N01018.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01018.definitions: a pathological change marked by inhibited movement of nutrient-yin qi, the same as stagnated nutrient-yin

V021-N01019.label: 虚
V021-N01019.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01019.definitions: (1) deficiency of the healthy qi; (2) weak constitution; (3) weak reaction against pathogens

V021-N01020.label: 虚中夹实
V021-N01020.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01020.definitions: deficiency condition complicated by excess symptoms with the former dominant

V021-N01021.label: 虚实
V021-N01021.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01021.definitions: one of the guiding principles for analyzing the condition of the body’s resistance to pathogenic factors, in which deficiency refers to deficiency of the healthy qi and excess refers to excessiveness of the pathogenic qi

V021-N01022.label: 虚实夹杂
V021-N01022.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01022.definitions: a pathological state in which both excess of pathogenic qi and debilitation of healthy qi occur in the disease process

V021-N01023.label: 虚实真假
V021-N01023.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01023.definitions: false appearance of deficiency-excess manifestations opposite to the true nature of the disease

V021-N01024.label: 虚寒
V021-N01024.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01024.definitions: a pathological change arising when yang qi becomes insufficient and fails to provide adequate warmth

V021-N01025.label: 虚火
V021-N01025.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01025.definitions: consumption of yin fluid failing to restrain yang fire, giving rise to fire of the deficiency type

V021-N01026.label: 虚火上炎
V021-N01026.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01026.definitions: a pathological change occurring in yin deficiency which is unable to inhibit yang, causing upflaming of the deficiency fire

V021-N01027.label: 虚阳上浮
V021-N01027.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01027.definitions: a pathological change in which consumption of essence and blood deprives yang of its base, causing yang to float at the upper surface

V021-N01028.label: 血不归经
V021-N01028.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01028.englishNames: blood failing to stay in the meridians
V021-N01028.definitions: a pathological change that causes extravasation of blood

V021-N01029.label: 血分热毒
V021-N01029.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01029.definitions: a pathological change characterized by the heat toxin penetrating deeply in the blood aspect, causing high fever with macular eruption, hemorrhages and even mental clouding

V021-N01030.label: 血寒
V021-N01030.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01030.definitions: a pathological change in which cold either externally contracted or originated from yang deficiency, entering the blood aspect, causes qi stagnation or blood stasis by its congealing effect

V021-N01031.label: 血热
V021-N01031.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01031.definitions: a pathological change in which exuberant heat or fire enters the blood, usually causing hemorrhage

V021-N01032.label: 血热妄行
V021-N01032.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01032.englishNames: frenetic movement of blood due to heat
V021-N01032.definitions: a pathological change in which heat forces extravasation of blood

V021-N01033.label: 血瘀
V021-N01033.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01033.definitions: a morbid state of blood stagnancy in a certain area of the body caused by sluggish flow of qi, deficiency of qi or blood, trauma, or yin-cold

V021-N01034.label: 血虚
V021-N01034.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01034.definitions: any pathological change characterized by deficiency of blood which fails to nourish organs, tissues and meridians/channels

V021-N01035.label: 血逆
V021-N01035.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01035.definitions: a pathological change characterized by reverse flow of blood in the meridian/channel causing separation of qi and blood

V021-N01036.label: 血随气逆
V021-N01036.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01036.definitions: a pathological change in which the upward adverse flow of qi leads blood rushing upward

V021-N01037.label: 表实
V021-N01037.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01037.definitions: invasion of external pathogens that causes gathering of defense qi in the skin and flesh and blockage of the interstices and pores marked by absence of sweating, chills and a floating forceful pulse

V021-N01038.label: 表实里虚
V021-N01038.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01038.definitions: a pathological state characterized by the simultaneous presence of exterior excess and interior deficiency.

V021-N01039.label: 表寒
V021-N01039.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01039.definitions: attack on the exterior part of the body by windcold, chiefly manifested by severe aversion to wind and cold, headache and stiff neck, soreness of the limbs and joints, thin white coating of the tongue and floating tense pulse

V021-N01040.label: 表寒里热
V021-N01040.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01040.definitions: a pathological state characterized by the simultaneous presence of exterior cold and interior heat

V021-N01041.label: 表气不固
V021-N01041.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01041.definitions: failure of insufficient defense qi to protect the superficies of the body against external pathogens, the same as insecurity of defense qi

V021-N01042.label: 表热
V021-N01042.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01042.definitions: attack on the exterior part of the body by wind-heat, chiefly manifested by slight aversion to wind and cold, moderate fever, headache, slight thirst, thin white or thin yellowish coating of the tongue, or red tip of the tongue and floating rapid puls

V021-N01043.label: 表热里寒
V021-N01043.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01044.label: 表虚
V021-N01044.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01044.definitions: deficiency of defense qi in the superficial part of the body, marked by spontaneous sweating or sweating accompanied by aversion to wind and floating feeble pulse

V021-N01045.label: 表虚里实
V021-N01045.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01045.definitions: a pathological state characterized by the simultaneous presence of exterior deficiency and interior excess

V021-N01046.label: 表邪入里
V021-N01046.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01046.definitions: the process of a disease whereby the external pathogen attacks the exterior first, and then enters the interior, impairing visceral functions

V021-N01047.label: 表邪内陷
V021-N01047.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01047.definitions: penetration of an externally contracted pathogen from the exterior into the interior

V021-N01048.label: 表里
V021-N01048.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01048.definitions: (1) the outer part of the body (the skin, body hair, flesh and superficial meridians/channels) and the inner part of the body (the bowels and viscera, qi, blood, and bone marrow); (2) two of the eight principles of pattern identification/syndrome diff

V021-N01049.label: 表里俱实
V021-N01049.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01049.englishNames: dual excess of the exterior and interior
V021-N01049.definitions: a pathological state characterized by the simultaneous presence of exterior excess and interior excess

V021-N01050.label: 表里俱寒
V021-N01050.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01050.englishNames: cold in both exterior and interior
V021-N01050.definitions: a pathological state characterized by the simultaneous presence of exterior cold and interior cold

V021-N01051.label: 表里俱热
V021-N01051.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01051.englishNames: heat in both exterior and interior
V021-N01051.definitions: a pathological state characterized by the simultaneous presence of exterior heat and interior heat

V021-N01052.label: 表里俱虚
V021-N01052.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01052.englishNames: dual deficiency of the exterior and interior
V021-N01052.definitions: a pathological state characterized by the simultaneous presence of exterior deficiency and interior deficiency

V021-N01053.label: 表里同病
V021-N01053.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01053.englishNames: dual disease of the exterior and interior
V021-N01053.definitions: coexistence of diseases both in the exterior and interior

V021-N01054.label: 越经传
V021-N01054.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01054.definitions: transmission of a cold-damage disease from one meridian/channel to another with skipping of one or more meridians/channels, e.g., transmission from greater yang to lesser yang with yang brightness skipped over

V021-N01055.label: 逆传
V021-N01055.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01055.definitions: transmission of a febrile disease contrary to the normal sequence, e.g., from the defense aspect directly to the nutrient aspect and blood aspect, instead of by way of the qi aspect, the same as non-sequential transmission

V021-N01056.label: 逆传心包
V021-N01056.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01056.definitions: the impairment of consciousness or coma occurring soon after the onset of an acute febrile disease, whereby the warm pathogen directly enters the pericardium without passing through the qi aspect

V021-N01057.label: 逆证
V021-N01057.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01058.label: 邪正盛衰
V021-N01058.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01058.englishNames: exuberance and debilitation of the healthy qi or pathogenic qi
V021-N01058.definitions: the key factor that determines the process of disease and prognosis, namely, rise of the healthy qi with decline of the pathogenic qi leading to improvement and cure, while exuberance of the pathogenic qi with debilitation of the healthy qi resulting

V021-N01059.label: 里实
V021-N01059.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01059.definitions: (1) a pathological change resulting from an external pathogen transforming into heat and entering the interior to bind in the stomach and intestines; (2) a general term denoting accumulation of pathological products in the body, such as phlegm, retain

V021-N01060.label: 里寒
V021-N01060.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01060.definitions: a pathological state characterized by preponderance of yin cold or decline of yang qi in the interior

V021-N01061.label: 里热
V021-N01061.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01061.definitions: a pathological state characterized by heat in the interior, either due to exuberance of pathogenic heat or due to yin deficiency with endogenous heat

V021-N01062.label: 里病出表
V021-N01062.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01062.englishNames: interior disease moving out to the exterior
V021-N01062.definitions: the process of a disease turning from the interior to the exterior through the struggle of the healthy qi against pathogens

V021-N01063.label: 里虚
V021-N01063.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01063.definitions: a general term for deficiency of qi, blood, yin and yang of the internal organs

V021-N01064.label: 金实不鸣
V021-N01064.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01064.definitions: an expression figuratively referring to hoarseness or loss of voice due to excessiveness of lung qi

V021-N01065.label: 金破不鸣
V021-N01065.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01065.definitions: an expression figuratively referring to hoarseness due to deficiency of lung qi

V021-N01066.label: 阳亡阴竭
V021-N01066.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01066.englishNames: collapse of yang and exhaustion of yin
V021-N01066.definitions: collapse of both yin and yang, indicating the moribund state of a critically ill patient

V021-N01067.label: 阳损及阴
V021-N01067.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01067.definitions: a pathological change in which weakness of yang qi impedes the generation of yin, resulting in deficiency of both yin and yang with a preponderance of yang deficiency

V021-N01068.label: 阳盛
V021-N01068.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01068.definitions: a pathological state in which yang is exuberant while yin has not been debilitated, giving rise to an excessheat syndrome, the same as yang excess

V021-N01069.label: 阳盛阴衰
V021-N01069.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01069.definitions: a pathological state characterized by simultaneous presence of exuberance of yang and debilitation of yin, also the same as yang excess with yin deficiency

V021-N01070.label: 阳虚
V021-N01070.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01070.definitions: a pathological state characterized by deficiency of body’s yang qi that leads to diminished functions, decreased metabolic activities, reduced body reactions as well as deficiency-cold manifestations; in Kampo medicine, “yang” and “excess” are indepen

V021-N01071.label: 阳虚阴盛
V021-N01071.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01071.definitions: insufficient yang failing to counterbalance yin, resulting in relative exuberance of yin, the same as yang deficiency with yin excess

V021-N01072.label: 阴损及阳
V021-N01072.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01072.definitions: a pathological change in which consumption of yin weakens yang qi, resulting in deficiency of both yin and yang with a preponderance of yin deficiency

V021-N01073.label: 阴盛
V021-N01073.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01073.definitions: a pathological state in which yin pathogen is exuberant while the healthy qi has not been damaged, giving rise to an excess-cold syndrome, the same as yin excess

V021-N01074.label: 阴盛格阳
V021-N01074.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01074.definitions: a pathological state in which extremely excessive yin entrenched in the exterior forces the asthenic yang to float on the body surface, leading to pseudo-heat symptoms, the same as excessive yin repelling yang, and also known as repelled yang

V021-N01075.label: 阴盛格阴
V021-N01075.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01076.label: 阴盛阳衰
V021-N01076.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01076.definitions: exuberant yin cold with resultant debilitation of yang qi, the same as yin excess with yang deficiency

V021-N01077.label: 阴竭阳脱
V021-N01077.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01077.englishNames: exhaustion of yin and collapse of yang
V021-N01077.definitions: exhaustion of yin fluid and collapse of yang qi with resultant functional failure, indicating that the patient is critically ill

V021-N01078.label: 阴虚
V021-N01078.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01078.definitions: a pathological change marked by deficiency of yin with diminished moistening, calming, downbearing and yang-inhibiting function, leading to relative hyperactivity of yang qi; in Kampo medicine, “yin” and “deficiency” are independently understood

V021-N01079.label: 阴虚内热
V021-N01079.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01079.definitions: insufficient yin failing to counterbalance yang, causing deficiency-heat to arise internally

V021-N01080.label: 阴虚火旺
V021-N01080.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01080.definitions: insufficient yin failing to restrain yang, causing exuberant fire of the deficiency type

V021-N01081.label: 阴虚阳亢
V021-N01081.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01081.definitions: insufficient essence, blood and fluid failing to restrain yang, causing increased activity of yang

V021-N01082.label: 阴阳两虚
V021-N01082.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01082.englishNames: dual deficiency of yin and yang
V021-N01082.definitions: a pathological state characterized by deficiency of both yin and yang

V021-N01083.label: 阴阳偏盛

V021-N01083.definitions: any pathological change marked by yin or yang higher than the normal level due to yin pathogens or yang pathogens

V021-N01084.label: 阴阳偏衰
V021-N01084.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01084.englishNames: abnormal debilitation of yin or yang
V021-N01084.definitions: any pathological change marked by yin or yang lower than the normal level due to deficiency of yin or yang of the human body

V021-N01085.label: 阴阳失调
V021-N01085.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01085.definitions: a general term for all kinds of pathological changes due to imbalance and incoordination of yin and yang

V021-N01087.label: 阴阳离决

V021-N01089.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01090.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01097.label: 食复
V021-N01097.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01230.definitions: the divergent passage of a regular meridian going deep in the body

V021-N01231.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01233.definitions: a theory concerned the meridians and collaterals, the same as channels and networks theory

V021-N01245.label: 阳维脉
V021-N01245.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01245.englishNames: yang link vessel; Yang Link Channel
V021-N01245.definitions: one of the eight extra meridians which originates from the lower part of the outer ankle running upward along the lateral side of the lower limb, through the side of the trunk, the shoulder, neck and terminating at the top of the head

V021-N01249.label: 脏腑类
V021-N01249.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01250.label: 三焦
V021-N01250.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01250.definitions: a collective term for the three portions of the body cavity, through which the visceral qi is transformed, also widely known as triple burners

V021-N01251.label: 上焦
V021-N01251.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01251.definitions: the chest cavity, i.e., the portion above the diaphragm housing the heart and lung, also known as upper burner

V021-N01252.label: 下焦
V021-N01252.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01252.definitions: the lower abdominal cavity, i.e., the portion below the umbilicus housing the kidneys, bladder, small and large intestines, also known as lower burner

V021-N01253.label: 中焦
V021-N01253.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01253.definitions: the upper abdominal cavity, i.e., the portion between the diaphragm and the umbilicus housing the spleen, stomach, liver and gallbladder, also known as middle burner

V021-N01254.label: 五脏
V021-N01254.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01254.definitions: a collective term for the heart, liver, spleen, lung and kidney

V021-N01255.label: 元神之府
V021-N01255.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01256.label: 先天
V021-N01256.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01257.label: 先天之本
V021-N01257.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01257.definitions: that which is inherited at conception

V021-N01258.label: 六腑
V021-N01258.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01258.definitions: a collective term for the gallbladder, stomach, large intestine, small intestine, urinary bladder and triple energizers

V021-N01259.label: 刚脏
V021-N01259.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01259.definitions: an internal organ that has a tendency to hyperactivity and counterflow of qi, referring to the liver, also known as resolute viscus

V021-N01260.label: 升发
V021-N01260.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01260.definitions: the upward and outward movement of qi, a function governed by the liver, the same as sending the qi upwards, outwards and throughout the body

V021-N01261.label: 升清
V021-N01261.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01261.definitions: referring to the function of spleen that sends the food essence to the heart and lung, the same as sending the clear upward

V021-N01262.label: 呼吸
V021-N01262.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01262.definitions: drawing air into the lung and expelling it to perform gas exchange, the same as respiration

V021-N01263.label: 大肠
V021-N01263.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01263.definitions: one of the six bowels, which receives waste passed down from the small intestine and then forms it into stool before discharging it from the body

V021-N01264.label: 天癸
V021-N01264.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01264.definitions: that upon which development of the reproductive organs and maintenance of reproductive function depends, derived from the kidney essence when it is abundant, also called (female) sex-stimulating essence/menstruation

V021-N01265.label: 奇恒之腑
V021-N01265.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01265.definitions: a collective term for the brain, marrow, bones, blood vessels, gallbladder and uterus. They are called extraordinary because their morphological and physiological properties are different from the ordinary bowels and viscera

V021-N01266.label: 娇脏
V021-N01266.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01266.definitions: an expression referring to the lung which is the viscus most susceptible to invasion by external pathogens

V021-N01267.label: 宣发
V021-N01267.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01267.definitions: the upward and outward movement of lung qi, also known as dispersion

V021-N01268.label: 小肠
V021-N01268.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01268.definitions: one of the six bowels, whose main function is to receive food content of the stomach, further digest it and absorb nutrients and water

V021-N01269.label: 心
V021-N01269.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01269.definitions: the organ located in the thoracic cavity above the diaphragm, which controls blood circulation and mental activities

V021-N01270.label: 心包
V021-N01270.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01270.aliases: 心包; 心包络; 膻中
V021-N01270.definitions: the outer covering of the heart, including the pericardium collateral

V021-N01271.label: 心开窍于舌
V021-N01271.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01271.definitions: the heart opens into the tongue, reflecting its physiological and pathological conditions

V021-N01272.label: 心肾相交
V021-N01272.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01272.definitions: superior-inferior, ascending-descending, water-fire and yin-yang coordination between the heart and kidney

V021-N01273.label: 心血
V021-N01273.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01273.definitions: the blood governed by the heart, which flows all over the body, particularly that part of blood serving as the basis for the physiological activities of the heart, including mental activities

V021-N01274.label: 心阳
V021-N01274.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01274.definitions: yang qi of the heart, which stimulates the activities of the heart and mind, and has a warming action

V021-N01275.label: 心阴
V021-N01275.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01275.definitions: yin essence of the heart, the opposite of heart yang, the quiescent and moistening aspect of heart function

V021-N01276.label: 水之上源
V021-N01276.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01276.definitions: an expression referring to the lung, which is situated in the upper energizer regulating water metabolism

V021-N01277.label: 水道
V021-N01277.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01277.definitions: the pathway of water metabolism in the body

V021-N01278.label: 治节
V021-N01278.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01278.definitions: the function of the lung to keep the physiological activity of the whole body in balance

V021-N01279.label: 泌别清浊
V021-N01279.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01279.englishNames: separation of the clear and turbid
V021-N01279.definitions: the small intestine’s function, by which the clear (the food essence and water) is absorbed while the turbid (the waste matter) is passed to the large intestine

V021-N01280.label: 生化
V021-N01280.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01280.definitions: referring to formation of qi and blood from food essence that is closely related to the spleen function, also known as generation and transformation

V021-N01281.label: 生殖
V021-N01281.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01281.definitions: the process by which humans give rise to offspring

V021-N01282.label: 疏泄
V021-N01282.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01282.definitions: referring to the function of the liver that ensures the free movement of qi and prevents qi stagnation, also known as soothing

V021-N01283.label: 神明
V021-N01283.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01283.definitions: all the human life activities including mind, will, mood and thinking, governed by the heart

V021-N01284.label: 纳气
V021-N01284.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01284.definitions: the action of the kidney in connection with normal respiration. The lung sends the qi down to the kidney, which holds the qi down, and then absorbs it

V021-N01285.label: 肃降
V021-N01285.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01285.definitions: the downward movement and purifying action of lung qi in contrast to its diffusing action

V021-N01286.label: 肝
V021-N01286.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01286.definitions: the organ located in the right hypochondrium below the diaphragm, which stores blood, facilitates the coursing of qi, and is closely related to the function of the sinews and eyes

V021-N01287.label: 肝开窍于目
V021-N01287.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01287.definitions: the liver opens into the eyes, reflecting its physiological and pathological conditions

V021-N01288.label: 肝肾同源
V021-N01288.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01288.definitions: the liver and the kidney are intimately related, as they nourish and support each other, the blood stored in the liver and the essence stored in the kidney are of the same origin, and the ministerial fire present in both the liver and the kidney is fr

V021-N01289.label: 肝血
V021-N01289.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01289.definitions: the blood that is stored in the liver and nourishes the liver system, including the liver itself, liver meridian, eyes, tendons and nails, as well as the whole body

V021-N01290.label: 肝阳
V021-N01290.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01290.definitions: yang qi of the liver, opposite to liver yin, referring chiefly to the warming, upbearing and coursingfreeing function of the liver

V021-N01291.label: 肝阴

V021-N01296.label: 肾

V021-N01296.definitions: a pair of organs located in the lumbar region, which store vital essence, promote growth, development, reproduction, and urinary function, and also have a direct effect on the condition of the bone and marrow, activities of the brain, hearing and insp

V021-N01297.definitions: the kidney opens into the ears, reflecting its physiological and pathological conditions

V021-N01298.label: 肾阳
V021-N01298.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01298.definitions: the yang aspect of the kidney, which warms and activates all organs

V021-N01299.label: 肾阴
V021-N01299.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01299.definitions: the yang aspect of the kidney, which warms and activates all organs

V021-N01300.label: 胃
V021-N01300.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01300.definitions: one of the six bowels, whose main function is to receive and initiate food digestion

V021-N01301.label: 胃津

V021-N01302.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01309.label: 脏腑

V021-N01310.label: 脏象

V021-N01313.label: 脾

V021-N01313.definitions: the organ located in the middle energizer below the diaphragm, whose main function is to transport and transform food, upbear the clear substances, keep the blood flowing within the vessels, and is closely related to the limbs and flesh

V021-N01314.label: 脾开窍于口

V021-N01314.definitions: the spleen opens into the mouth, reflecting its physiological and pathological conditions

V021-N01316.label: 脾阴

V021-N01318.label: 膀胱
V021-N01318.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01319.label: 膀胱气化

V021-N01320.label: 血室
V021-N01320.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01321.label: 血海

V021-N01333.label: 总论

V021-N01333.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01335.label: 中医养生学

V021-N01346.label: 体质

V021-N01346.definitions: the characteristics of an individual, including structural and functional characteristics, temperament, adaptability to environmental changes and susceptibility to disease. It is relatively stable, being in part, genetically determined and in part, acqu

V021-N01351.label: 四象人

V021-N01357.label: 少阴人
V021-N01357.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01361.label: 李朱医学

V021-N01362.label: 汉方
V021-N01362.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01363.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01368.label: 蒙医学
V021-N01368.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01369.label: 藏医学
V021-N01369.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01370.label: 越医学
V021-N01370.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01370.definitions: the medicine traditionally practiced in Vietnam, based on ancient Chinese medicine

V021-N01371.label: 韩医学
V021-N01371.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01372.label: 治疗学
V021-N01372.parentName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01372.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01373.label: 治则
V021-N01373.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01373.definitions: a general rule that should be followed in treating disease

V021-N01376.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01380.label: 塞因塞用
V021-N01380.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01381.label: 实则泻之

V021-N01473.label: 弹筋法

V021-N01476.label: 托疮

V021-N01491.label: 推法
V021-N01491.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01734.label: 镇惊安神
V021-N01734.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01734.englishNames: settle fright and tranquilize; calming fright and tranquilizing mind
V021-N01734.definitions: a therapeutic method of using settling tranquilizers to relieve mental uneasiness caused by fright

V021-N01735.label: 镇肝熄风
V021-N01735.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01735.englishNames: calm the liver to extinguish wind
V021-N01735.definitions: a therapeutic method of treating internal wind caused by hyperactivity of the liver

V021-N01736.label: 降气化痰
V021-N01736.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01736.englishNames: direct qi downward to resolve phlegm
V021-N01736.definitions: a therapeutic method of combined use of qidownbearing medicinals and phlegm-resolving medicinals to treat reverse flow of qi due to phlegm obstruction

V021-N01737.label: 降气止呃
V021-N01737.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01737.aliases: 降气止呃; 降逆止呃
V021-N01737.englishNames: direct qi downward to relieve hiccup; descending qi and relieving hiccup
V021-N01737.definitions: a therapeutic method of treating hiccups

V021-N01738.label: 降火
V021-N01738.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01738.definitions: a therapeutic method of using heat-clearing and firepurging medicinals to treat upward invasion of heatfire

V021-N01739.label: 降逆下气
V021-N01739.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01739.definitions: a therapeutic method of treating upward counterflow of qi in the lung and stomach manifested as cough, asthma, hiccupping or vomiting

V021-N01740.label: 降逆止咳平喘
V021-N01740.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01740.englishNames: downbear counterflow to suppress cough and to calm panting
V021-N01740.definitions: a therapeutic method for treating counterflow of lung qi with cough and dyspnea

V021-N01741.label: 除疳热
V021-N01741.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01741.definitions: a therapeutic method for treating malnutritional fever in children

V021-N01742.label: 驱虫
V021-N01742.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01742.definitions: a therapeutic method of expelling intestinal parasites

V021-N01743.label: 药物治疗
V021-N01743.parentName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01743.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01744.label: 方剂类
V021-N01744.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01745.label: 临睡服
V021-N01745.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01746.label: 丸剂
V021-N01746.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01746.definitions: a solid globular mass, coated or uncoated, made of finely powdered medicinals with a suitable excipient or binder

V021-N01747.label: 丹剂
V021-N01747.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01747.definitions: a medicated preparation in the form of small particles, usually made from minerals by sublimation for topical application, but some also for internal administration

V021-N01748.label: 主治
V021-N01748.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01748.definitions: major symptom or particular condition that indicate the advisability or necessity of a specific medical treatment or procedure, also called major indication

V021-N01749.label: 代茶服
V021-N01749.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01749.definitions: to be taken in frequent small amounts as drinking tea

V021-N01750.label: 佐药
V021-N01750.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01750.definitions: the ingredient that treats the combined pattern/ syndrome, relieves secondary symptoms or tempers the action of the sovereign ingredient when the latter is too potent

V021-N01751.label: 使药
V021-N01751.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01751.definitions: the ingredient that directs action to the affected meridian/channel or site

V021-N01752.label: 偶方
V021-N01752.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01752.definitions: a formula or prescription with ingredients even in number

V021-N01753.label: 先煎
V021-N01753.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01753.definitions: boil certain medicinals before other medicinals are added while making a decoction

V021-N01754.label: 八阵
V021-N01754.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01755.label: 冲剂
V021-N01755.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01755.definitions: granules made of medicinal extract, usually with sugar, to be dissolved in boiling water before being taken

V021-N01756.label: 冲服
V021-N01756.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01756.definitions: take medicine after pouring hot water or hot decoction of other medicinals over it, with stirring

V021-N01757.label: 冷服
V021-N01757.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01758.label: 利湿剂
V021-N01758.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01758.definitions: formula composed of dampness-draining diuretics, used to treat exuberant dampness

V021-N01759.label: 剂型
V021-N01759.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01759.definitions: form of a prepared medicine designed according to the medicinal property, therapeutic purpose and route of administration

V021-N01760.label: 包煎
V021-N01760.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01761.label: 反佐
V021-N01761.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01761.definitions: an assistant ingredient with a property opposite to and a therapeutic effect complementary to the sovereign ingredient

V021-N01762.label: 发表剂
V021-N01762.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01763.label: 另煎
V021-N01763.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01763.definitions: boil the medicinal separately in order to avoid absorption of the extract by the dregs of other medicinals

V021-N01764.label: 后下
V021-N01764.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01764.definitions: add certain medicinals for boiling when the decoction is nearly done

V021-N01765.label: 君臣佐使
V021-N01765.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01765.definitions: the ingredients in a formula or prescription that have different roles

V021-N01766.label: 君药
V021-N01766.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01766.definitions: the ingredient that provides the principal curative action on the main pattern/syndrome or primary symptom

V021-N01767.label: 和解剂

V021-N01768.label: 固涩剂

V021-N01769.label: 坐药

V021-N01770.label: 复方
V021-N01770.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01770.definitions: a formula or prescription that is formed by multiple ingredients or by two or more set formulas

V021-N01771.label: 大方

V021-N01772.label: 奇方
V021-N01772.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01772.definitions: a formula or prescription with ingredients odd in number

V021-N01773.label: 安神剂
V021-N01773.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01773.definitions: formula that calms or tranquilizes excitement, used for treating disquieted spirit

V021-N01774.label: 寒下剂

V021-N01774.englishNames: cold purgative formula; Cold Cathartic Formula
V021-N01774.definitions: a purgative formula cold in nature, indicated in the treatment of interior accumulation and stagnation of heat

V021-N01775.label: 小方

V021-N01775.definitions: a formula or prescription which contains only a few ingredients of small dosage, used for treating a mild illness

V021-N01776.label: 平旦服
V021-N01776.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01777.label: 开窍剂
V021-N01777.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01778.label: 急方
V021-N01778.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01778.definitions: a formula or prescription which is employed for immediate effect in the treatment of emergency or critical cases

V021-N01779.label: 攻里剂
V021-N01779.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01779.definitions: any formula mainly composed of purgatives with bowel-moving, heat-purging, accumulation-attacking and water-discharging effects, used for treating interior excess patterns/syndromes

V021-N01780.label: 救急剂
V021-N01780.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01780.definitions: any formula used in emergency cases

V021-N01781.label: 散剂
V021-N01781.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01782.label: 文火
V021-N01782.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01783.label: 方剂
V021-N01783.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01784.label: 明目剂
V021-N01784.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01784.definitions: a general term for formulas used in the treatment of eye diseases, also called eye-brightening formula

V021-N01785.label: 杀虫剂
V021-N01785.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01785.definitions: any formula that kills parasitic worms

V021-N01786.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01788.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01789.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01794.label: 流膏

V021-N01795.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01795.definitions: a concentrated medicated preparation obtained by extracting the active constituents

V021-N01796.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01800.label: 清暑剂
V021-N01800.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01800.definitions: formula that cleans summerheat for treating heat patterns/syndromes occurring in summer

V021-N01801.label: 清热剂

V021-N01803.label: 温补剂
V021-N01803.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01803.definitions: any formula with warm-tonifying effect used for treating cold-deficiency conditions

V021-N01804.label: 溶化
V021-N01804.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01806.label: 片剂

V021-N01807.label: 理气剂
V021-N01807.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01808.label: 理法方药
V021-N01808.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01808.definitions: the four basic steps of diagnosis and treatment: determining the cause, mechanism and location of the disease according to the medical theories and principles, then deciding the treatment principle and method, and finally selecting a formula as well a

V021-N01809.label: 理血剂

V021-N01810.label: 痈疡剂

V021-N01810.definitions: a general term for formulas used to treat sores, abscesses and ulcers in external medicine

V021-N01811.label: 祛寒剂

V021-N01812.label: 祛湿剂
V021-N01812.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01813.label: 祛痰剂

V021-N01816.label: 经产剂

V021-N01817.label: 经方

V021-N01818.label: 缓方

V021-N01819.label: 膏剂
V021-N01819.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01820.label: 膏药

V021-N01821.label: 臣药
V021-N01821.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01821.definitions: the ingredient that helps strengthen the principal curative action

V021-N01822.label: 茶剂
V021-N01822.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01824.definitions: a twisted paper like a thread, coated with or enveloping medicated powder

V021-N01825.definitions: a preparation made by steeping medicinals in wine or liquor

V021-N01826.label: 蜜丸
V021-N01826.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01826.definitions: a globular medicated mass, in which processed honey is used as a binder

V021-N01827.label: 蜡丸

V021-N01828.label: 补益剂
