V021-N01828.definitions: any formula that reinforces yang, replenishes qi, nourishes blood or supplements yin in deficiency conditions

V021-N01829.label: 表里双解剂
V021-N01829.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01829.englishNames: exterior-interiorreleasing formula; Formula for Relieving both Superficial and Internal Disorders
V021-N01829.definitions: any formula that eliminates pathogens from both the exterior and the interior parts of the body, indicated in the treatment of dual disease of the exterior and interior

V021-N01830.label: 解表剂
V021-N01830.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01830.definitions: formula to dispel pathogenic factors from the superficies of the body for the treatment of exterior patterns/syndromes

V021-N01831.label: 软膏
V021-N01831.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01831.definitions: an unguent (ointment) for topical application to the skin

V021-N01832.label: 针剂
V021-N01832.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01832.definitions: a preparation of medicine intended for injection

V021-N01833.label: 顿服
V021-N01833.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01833.definitions: (decoction) to be taken all in one single dose

V021-N01834.label: 频服
V021-N01834.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01834.definitions: (decoction) to be taken in small portions at frequent intervals

V021-N01835.label: 食远服
V021-N01835.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01835.definitions: to be taken at a longer interval from meal

V021-N01836.label: 饭前服
V021-N01836.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01836.definitions: to be taken about one hour before meal

V021-N01837.label: 饭后服
V021-N01837.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01837.definitions: to be taken about one hour after meal

V021-N01838.label: 饮片
V021-N01838.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01838.definitions: medicinal substances in small pieces or slices for decocting, mostly after appropriate processing, also called medicinal slices

V021-N01839.label: 驱虫剂
V021-N01839.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01839.definitions: any formula that expels parasitic worms

V021-N01840.label: 药(物)
V021-N01840.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01840.definitions: a medicinal substance having or designating healing or curative properties or attributes

V021-N01841.label: 上品
V021-N01841.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01841.definitions: medicinals that are non-toxic, possesses a rejuvenating effect and can be taken frequently and for a long period of time without harm

V021-N01842.label: 下品
V021-N01842.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01842.definitions: medicinals that are effective for expelling pathogens, but are toxic and should not be taken for a long period of time

V021-N01843.label: 中品
V021-N01843.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01843.definitions: medicinals that have no or only slight toxic effect and are effective for treating diseases or deficiency conditions

V021-N01844.label: 中药
V021-N01844.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01844.definitions: usually referring to those medicinal substances recorded in Chinese materia medica

V021-N01845.label: 五味
V021-N01845.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01845.definitions: the five tastes of medicinals, pungency, sweetness, sourness, bitterness, and saltiness, representing the basic actions of the medicinals

V021-N01846.label: 养心安神药
V021-N01846.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01846.definitions: a medicinal that nourishes yin-blood of the heart to calm the mind

V021-N01847.label: 凉血止血药
V021-N01847.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01847.definitions: a medicinal that arrests bleeding by clearing the blood of heat, indicated in the treatment of bloodheat hemorrhage

V021-N01848.label: 切(制)
V021-N01848.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01849.label: 利尿通淋药
V021-N01849.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01849.definitions: a medicinal that increases urine excretion and relieves strangury, mainly indicated in the treatment of dampness-heat in the lower energizer with difficult and painful discharge of urine

V021-N01850.label: 利水消肿药
V021-N01850.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01850.definitions: a medicinal that increases urine excretion for treating edema

V021-N01851.label: 利水渗湿药
V021-N01851.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01851.definitions: a medicinal that increases urine excretion and water discharge for treating internal retention of dampness

V021-N01852.label: 利湿退黄药
V021-N01852.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01852.definitions: a medicinal that drains dampness and relieves icterus, indicated in the treatment of dampness-heat jaundice

V021-N01853.label: 利胆退黄药
V021-N01853.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01853.definitions: a medicinal that promotes the discharge of bile to relieve jaundice

V021-N01854.label: 剂量
V021-N01854.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01854.definitions: the measured quantity of a medicinal to be taken

V021-N01855.label: 剑肺涩肠药
V021-N01855.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01855.definitions: a sour and astringent medicinal that relieves cough and dyspnea, and arrests chronic diarrhea

V021-N01856.label: 化湿药
V021-N01856.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01856.definitions: a medicinal with fragrant odor, warming and drying, that resolves dampness

V021-N01857.label: 化瘀止血药
V021-N01857.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01857.definitions: a medicinal that arrests bleeding and at the same time removes stagnant blood which may cause further bleeding

V021-N01858.label: 化瘀药
V021-N01858.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01858.definitions: a category of medicinals used in the treatment of blood stasis

V021-N01859.label: 十九畏
V021-N01859.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01859.definitions: medicinals of mutual restraint which, if used in combination, one may restrain or neutralize the other’s action: sulfur being incompatible with crude sodium sulfate; mercury being incompatible arsenic trioxide; Radix Euphorbiae Ebracteolatae being inc

V021-N01860.label: 十八反
V021-N01860.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01860.definitions: incompatible medicinals which, if given in combination, are believed to have serious side effects: Radix Glycyrrhizae being antagonistic to Radix Euphorbiae Pekinensis, Flos Genkwa, Radix Euphorbiae Kansui and Sargassum; Radix Aconiti being antagonist

V021-N01861.label: 升降浮沉
V021-N01861.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01861.definitions: direction of medicinal action, upward, downward, outward, and inward

V021-N01862.label: 去火毒
V021-N01862.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01862.definitions: remove irritants from newly prepared medicinal plasters

V021-N01863.label: 发散风寒药
V021-N01863.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01863.definitions: a medicinal that has the effect of dispersing wind and cold in the treatment of an exterior pattern/syndrome

V021-N01864.label: 发散风热药
V021-N01864.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01864.definitions: a medicinal that has the effect of dispersing wind and heat in the treatment of an exterior pattern/syndrome

V021-N01865.label: 四气
V021-N01865.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01865.englishNames: four qi; four properties; four nature of drugs
V021-N01865.definitions: the four basic properties of medicinals, cold, hot, warmth, and coolness

V021-N01866.label: 固表止汗药
V021-N01866.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01866.definitions: a medicinal that arrests excessive sweating by strengthening the superficies

V021-N01867.label: 妊娠禁忌
V021-N01867.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01867.definitions: medicinals whose administration is prohibited during pregnancy

V021-N01868.label: 安神药
V021-N01868.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01868.definitions: a medicinal that calms the mind and relieves mental tension

V021-N01869.label: 峻下逐水药
V021-N01869.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01869.definitions: a cathartic that causes copious water discharge for reducing accumulated fluid in anasarca, ascites and pleural effusion

V021-N01870.label: 平肝熄风药
V021-N01870.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01870.definitions: a medicinal that pacifies the liver, suppresses exuberant yang, extinguishes internal wind and controls spasms or tremors

V021-N01871.label: 开窍药
V021-N01871.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01871.definitions: a medicinal, fragrant in flavor, with a resuscitating effect, used for emergency treatment of loss of consciousness in block pattern/syndrome

V021-N01872.label: 归经
V021-N01872.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01872.definitions: orientation of the medicinal action according to the meridian/channel on which the therapeutic action is manifested, also called meridian tropism

V021-N01873.label: 收敛止血药
V021-N01873.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01873.definitions: a medicinal that arrests bleeding by its astringent action

V021-N01874.label: 收涩药
V021-N01874.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01874.definitions: a variety of medicinals that arrest discharges due to insecure health qi and declined visceral function such as excessive sweating, chronic diarrhea, enuresis, bleeding, leukorrhea and spermatorrhea of deficiency type

V021-N01875.label: 攻下药
V021-N01875.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01875.definitions: a medicinal that has a potent purgative effect for moving the bowels and driving away excessive heat and stagnant matter

V021-N01876.label: 敛汗固表药
V021-N01876.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01876.definitions: a medicinal that strengthens the superficies by arresting excessive sweating

V021-N01877.label: 方寸匕
V021-N01877.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01877.definitions: an ancient instrument for measuring the quantity of medicinals, one square-inch-spoon equal to 2.74 ml, about 2 g of powdered mineral ingredients and 1 g of powdered herbal ingredients

V021-N01878.label: 服药食忌
V021-N01878.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01878.definitions: types of food that should be avoided during the period of medication

V021-N01879.label: 本草
V021-N01879.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01879.definitions: that aspect of medical science concerned with the origin and preparation of medicinal agents, their doses and method of administration, also refers to Chinese medicinal herbs

V021-N01880.label: 柔肝药
V021-N01880.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01880.definitions: a medicinal that replenishes yin-blood of the liver in treating deficiency of liver yin or blood

V021-N01881.label: 止咳平喘药
V021-N01881.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01881.definitions: a medicinal that relieves cough and dyspnea

V021-N01882.label: 止血药
V021-N01882.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01882.definitions: a category of medicinals that arrest bleeding, either internal or external

V021-N01883.label: 气味
V021-N01883.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01883.definitions: the property and flavor of a medicinal that represent the main effects of that medicinal

V021-N01884.label: 水制
V021-N01884.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01884.aliases: 水制; 漂制; 润制; 水飞制
V021-N01884.definitions: processing by utilizing water, including washing, bleaching, soaking, refining with water, etc.

V021-N01885.label: 水飞
V021-N01885.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01885.definitions: remove impurities from a powdered medicinal and at the same time obtaining finer powder by mixing it with water in a tank and allowing the supernatant turbid fluid to settle in another tank, and then collecting the deposit

V021-N01886.label: 泻下药
V021-N01886.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01886.definitions: a category of medicinals that promote defecation or even cause diarrhea, not only for relieving constipation, but also for driving stagnant matter, excessive heat and retained fluid out of the body

V021-N01887.label: 活血化瘀药
V021-N01887.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01887.definitions: a medicinal that promotes blood flow and resolves blood stasis

V021-N01888.label: 活血止痛药
V021-N01888.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01888.definitions: a medicinal that activates blood, moves qi and alleviates pain, indicated in the treatment of painful conditions caused by stagnation of qi and blood

V021-N01889.label: 活血疗伤药
V021-N01889.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01889.englishNames: blood-activating trauma-curing medicinal; blood-activating and trauma-curing medicinal
V021-N01889.definitions: a medicinal that activates blood, reduces swelling, arrests bleeding and promotes the healing of wounds and fractures

V021-N01890.label: 活血祛瘀药
V021-N01890.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01890.definitions: a medicinal that promotes blood flow and dispels blood stasis

V021-N01891.label: 活血药
V021-N01891.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01891.definitions: a category of medicinals used in the treatment of retarded or static blood flow

V021-N01892.label: 活血行气药
V021-N01892.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01892.definitions: a medicinal that activates blood and promotes the flow of qi, used primarily in treating stagnation of qi and blood

V021-N01893.label: 活血调经药
V021-N01893.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01893.englishNames: blood-activating menstruationregulating medicinal; blood-activating and menstruation-regulating medicinal
V021-N01893.definitions: a medicinal that activates blood and regulates menstruation for treating menstrual disorders, dysmenorrhea, amenorrhea and postpartum blood stagnation

V021-N01894.label: 消散药
V021-N01894.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01894.definitions: a medicinal that promotes subsidence or diminution of sores and ulcers

V021-N01895.label: 消食药
V021-N01895.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01895.definitions: a medicinal that aids digestion to eliminate accumulated undigested food

V021-N01896.label: 涌吐药
V021-N01896.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N01897.label: 润下药
V021-N01897.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01897.definitions: a medicinal that lubricates the intestinal tract to facilitate defecation

V021-N01898.label: 清炒
V021-N01898.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01898.definitions: bake a medicinal in a pan, with constant stirring and without adding any adjuvant

V021-N01899.label: 清热凉血药
V021-N01899.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01899.definitions: a medicinal that has the effect of eliminating pathogenic heat from the nutrient and blood aspects in cases of externally contracted febrile diseases

V021-N01900.label: 清热收涩药
V021-N01900.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01900.definitions: a medicinal that clears heat and checks discharge, used in the treatment of bleeding or exudation due to pathogenic heat

V021-N01901.label: 清热泻火药
V021-N01901.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01901.definitions: a medicinal that has the effect of clearing heat or purging fire from the interior

V021-N01902.label: 清热燥湿药
V021-N01902.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01902.definitions: a medicinal that is effective for eliminating heat and dampness

V021-N01903.label: 清热药
V021-N01903.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01903.definitions: a category of medicinals that have the effect of clearing up internal heat in cases of externally contracted febrile diseases or fever due to yin deficiency

V021-N01904.label: 清热解毒药
V021-N01904.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01904.definitions: a medicinal that counteracts heat toxins

V021-N01905.label: 清虚热药
V021-N01905.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01905.definitions: a medicinal that clears heat from deficiency conditions

V021-N01906.label: 温下药
V021-N01906.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01906.definitions: a medicinal warm in property, which relieves constipation caused by excessive cold stagnation

V021-N01907.label: 温经止血药
V021-N01907.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01907.englishNames: meridian-warming hemostatic medicinal; meridian; channel-warming hemostatic
V021-N01907.definitions: a medicinal that arrests bleeding by warming the meridians/channels

V021-N01908.label: 温里药
V021-N01908.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01908.definitions: a medicinal that warms the interior and expels internal cold

V021-N01909.label: 漂
V021-N01909.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01909.definitions: steep the materials in clean water that is continuously replaced to eliminate impurities

V021-N01910.label: 火制
V021-N01910.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01910.definitions: processing by utilizing heat or fire, including stirbaking, baking, calcining, etc.

V021-N01911.label: 炒炭
V021-N01911.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01911.definitions: bake a medicinal in a pan, with constant stirring, till it partly turns to charcoal

V021-N01912.label: 炒焦
V021-N01912.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01912.englishNames: stir-bake to brown; stir-frying to brown
V021-N01912.definitions: bake a medicinal in a pan, with constant stirring, till it turns brown

V021-N01913.label: 炒黄
V021-N01913.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01913.englishNames: stir-bake to yellow; stir-frying to yellow
V021-N01913.definitions: bake a medicinal in a pan, with constant stirring, till it turns yellow

V021-N01914.label: 炙
V021-N01914.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01914.definitions: bake, with stirring, a medicinal together with adjuvant until the latter is infiltrated into the medicinal

V021-N01915.label: 炮制
V021-N01915.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01915.aliases: 炮制; 修治; 修事
V021-N01915.englishNames: processing of medicinals; processing of materia medica; processing
V021-N01915.definitions: a general term for treating of medicinal substances by various means before their medical use

V021-N01916.label: 烘焙
V021-N01916.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01916.definitions: dry a medicinal over a slow fire

V021-N01917.label: 煅
V021-N01917.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01917.definitions: burn a medicinal on a fire to make it crispy, easy to crush

V021-N01918.label: 煨
V021-N01918.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01918.definitions: bake a medicinal wrapped in wet paper or coated with dough in hot ashes till the paper or coat turns black

V021-N01919.label: 理气药
V021-N01919.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01919.definitions: a medicinal that regulates qi movement to treat qi stagnation or adverse qi flow

V021-N01920.label: 相使
V021-N01920.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01920.definitions: two or more medicinals used in combination, one being the principal substance while the others play a subsidiary role to reinforce the action of the former

V021-N01921.label: 相反
V021-N01921.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N01921.definitions: antagonistic combination of two medicinals, if used together, one may prevent the therapeutic effect of the other and/or there may be adverse effects

V021-N01922.label: 相恶

V021-N02062.definitions: a pattern/syndrome caused by exuberant pathogenic heat flooding in the yang brightness meridian and spreading over the body but not yet inducing constipation, characteristically manifested by high fever without chills, profuse sweating, strong thirst

V021-N02063.label: 阳明腑证
V021-N02063.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02063.definitions: a pattern/syndrome caused by accumulation of pathogenic heat in the stomach and large intestine, manifested by tidal fever, abdominal distension, fullness and pain, constipation, agitation or even delirium

V021-N02064.label: 切诊类
V021-N02064.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02065.label: 七恶
V021-N02065.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02065.definitions: seven signs indicating a poor prognosis of external lesions, i.e., poor heart, poor liver, poor spleen, poor lung, poor kidney, deteriorated viscera, and exhausted qi and blood

V021-N02066.label: 三部九候
V021-N02066.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02066.definitions: (1) the upper, middle and lower arteries of the head, and the upper and lower limbs for pulse examination; (2) three sections of the radial artery at the wrist designated as inch, bar and cubit, each felt with light, moderate and heavy force to study

V021-N02067.label: 举,按,寻
V021-N02067.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02067.definitions: the three manipulations in pulse taking

V021-N02068.label: 五善
V021-N02068.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02068.definitions: five signs indicating a good prognosis of external lesions, i.e., good heart, good liver, good spleen, good lung and good kidney

V021-N02069.label: 人迎
V021-N02069.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02069.definitions: pulsation of the carotid artery, easily felt beside the laryngeal prominence

V021-N02070.label: 代脉
V021-N02070.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02070.definitions: a moderate weak pulse, pausing at regular intervals

V021-N02071.label: 伏脉
V021-N02071.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02071.definitions: a pulse which can only be felt upon pressing to the bone, located deeper than sunken pulse or even totally hidden

V021-N02072.label: 促脉
V021-N02072.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02072.definitions: a rapid pulse with irregular intermittence

V021-N02073.label: 偃刀脉
V021-N02073.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02073.definitions: a pulse like a knife with the blade pointing upward, i.e., fine, string-like, and extremely tight

V021-N02074.label: 切脉
V021-N02074.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02074.definitions: examine the pulsation of blood vessels by feeling with the fingertips

V021-N02075.label: 切诊
V021-N02075.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02075.definitions: one of the four diagnostic examinations, including touching and pressing the body surface with the hand or fingers

V021-N02076.label: 动脉
V021-N02076.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02076.definitions: a quick, jerky pulse, like a bouncing pea, slippery, rapid and forceful

V021-N02077.label: 单按
V021-N02077.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02077.definitions: taking the pulse at each of the three sections individually

V021-N02078.label: 反关脉
V021-N02078.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02078.englishNames: pulse on the back of the wrist
V021-N02078.definitions: an anatomical anomaly of the radial artery which makes the pulse beat felt on the back of the wrist

V021-N02079.label: 叩击法
V021-N02079.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02079.definitions: examination of bone fracture or bone diseases with the longitudinal force induced by knocking gently on the distal end of the limb

V021-N02080.label: 大脉
V021-N02080.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02080.definitions: a broad pulse with a bigger amplitude than normal

V021-N02081.label: 实脉
V021-N02081.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02081.definitions: a general term for a pulse felt forceful at all the three sections, cun/inch, guan/bar and chi/cubit, also called forceful pulse

V021-N02082.label: 寸关尺
V021-N02082.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02082.definitions: the three sections over the radial artery for feeling the pulse: The bar/guan is just central to the radial styloid at the wrist, where the tip of the physician’s middle finger is placed, the inch/cun is next to it on the distal side where the tip of

V021-N02083.label: 寸口
V021-N02083.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02083.definitions: the pulsation of the radial artery felt at the wrist

V021-N02084.label: 寸口诊法
V021-N02084.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02084.definitions: method of taking pulse at the radial artery of the wrist

V021-N02085.label: 屋漏脉
V021-N02085.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02085.definitions: a pulse that comes at long and irregular intervals, like water dripping from a leaky roof

V021-N02086.label: 布指
V021-N02086.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02086.definitions: positioning of fingers in pulse examination

V021-N02087.label: 平脉
V021-N02087.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02087.definitions: the pulse of a normal person

V021-N02088.label: 弦脉
V021-N02088.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02088.definitions: a straight, long and taut pulse, like a musical string to the touch

V021-N02089.label: 弱脉
V021-N02089.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02089.definitions: a pulse that is deep, soft, thin and forceless

V021-N02090.label: 弹石脉
V021-N02090.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02090.definitions: a sunken replete pulse that feels like flicking a stone with a finger

V021-N02091.label: 微脉
V021-N02091.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02091.definitions: a thready and soft pulse, scarcely perceptible

V021-N02092.label: 怪脉
V021-N02092.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02093.label: 总按
V021-N02093.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02093.definitions: taking the pulse of the three sections with three fingers simultaneously

V021-N02094.label: 指法
V021-N02094.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02094.definitions: the finger manipulation used in pulse examination

V021-N02095.label: 指目
V021-N02095.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02095.definitions: a form of pulse examination using only the tips of the fingers in contact with the pulse

V021-N02096.label: 按腧穴
V021-N02096.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02096.definitions: palpation of the body surface at the sites of acupuncture points which reflect the condition of the corresponding visceral organs

V021-N02097.label: 按诊
V021-N02097.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02097.definitions: examination of the body surface by touch with the hand or fingers

V021-N02098.label: 推寻
V021-N02098.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02098.definitions: pushing and moving the finger to examine the pulse

V021-N02099.label: 散脉
V021-N02099.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02100.label: 数脉
V021-N02100.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02100.definitions: a pulse with more than five or six beats to one cycle of the physician’s respiration, the same as tachycardia

V021-N02101.label: 斜飞脉
V021-N02101.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02101.definitions: an anatomical anomaly of the radial artery which makes the pulse beat felt running outwards from the cubit/chi to the back of the hand

V021-N02102.label: 沉脉
V021-N02102.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02102.definitions: a deeply located pulse which can only be felt when pressing hard, also called deep pulse

V021-N02103.label: 洪脉
V021-N02103.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02103.definitions: a pulse beating like dashing waves with forceful rising and gradual decline, also called flooding pulse

V021-N02104.label: 浮脉
V021-N02104.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02104.definitions: a superficially located pulse which can be felt by light touch and grows faint on hard pressure

V021-N02105.label: 涩脉
V021-N02105.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02105.definitions: a pulse coming and going unsmoothly with small, fine, slow joggling tempo like scraping bamboo with a knife

V021-N02106.label: 滑脉
V021-N02106.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02106.definitions: a pulse coming and going smoothly like beads rolling on a plate

V021-N02107.label: 濡脉

V021-N02108.label: 牢脉
V021-N02108.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02109.label: 疾脉
V021-N02109.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02109.definitions: a pulse having more than seven beats per respiration

V021-N02110.label: 病脉
V021-N02110.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02111.label: 真脏脉
V021-N02111.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02111.definitions: a pulse condition indicating exhaustion of visceral qi

V021-N02112.label: 短脉
V021-N02112.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02112.definitions: a pulse with beats of short duration, only felt at guan/ bar section

V021-N02113.definitions: a sudden change of pulse condition in a pregnant woman when she is about to give birth

V021-N02114.label: 紧脉
V021-N02114.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02114.definitions: a pulse feeling like a tightly stretched cord

V021-N02115.label: 细脉
V021-N02115.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02115.definitions: a pulse as thin as a silk thread, straight and soft, feeble yet always perceptible upon hard pressure, also called thin/thready pulse

V021-N02116.label: 结脉
V021-N02116.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02116.definitions: a moderate weak pulse, pausing at irregular intervals

V021-N02117.label: 缓脉
V021-N02117.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02117.definitions: a pulse with four beats to one cycle of the physician’s respiration, even and harmonious in its form

V021-N02118.label: 缓脉
V021-N02118.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02119.label: 胃,神,根
V021-N02119.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02119.definitions: the three qualities for a normal pulse: A normal pulse is regular, smooth and harmonious, indicating the presence of stomach qi. It is supple and powerful, indicating the presence of vitality. It can be felt on deep palpation, indicating the presence

V021-N02120.label: 脉无胃气
V021-N02120.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02120.definitions: a pulse that has lost its usual rhythm, frequency and evenness, indicating lack of stomach qi

V021-N02121.label: 脉暴出
V021-N02121.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02121.definitions: sudden throbbing of a hardly perceptible pulse, usually indicating a critical condition

V021-N02122.label: 脉气
V021-N02122.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02123.label: 脉诊
V021-N02123.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02123.definitions: examination of the pulse for making diagnosis

V021-N02124.label: 脉象
V021-N02124.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02125.label: 脉象主病
V021-N02125.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02125.definitions: a certain type of pulse indicates a particular disease

V021-N02126.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02127.label: 脉静
V021-N02127.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02127.definitions: pulse that becomes gentle, in the course of an illness, usually indicating improvement of the condition

V021-N02128.label: 腹诊
V021-N02128.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02128.definitions: examination of the chest and abdomen by touching and pressing in combination with other examinations for the purpose of determining a morbid condition in diagnosis

V021-N02129.label: 芤脉
V021-N02129.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02130.label: 虚脉
V021-N02130.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02131.label: 虾游脉
V021-N02131.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02132.label: 解索脉

V021-N02137.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02138.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02139.label: 釜沸脉

V021-N02141.label: 雀啄脉

V021-N02142.label: 革脉

V021-N02142.definitions: a pulse felt hard and hollow as if touching the surface of a drum

V021-N02143.label: 鱼翔脉
V021-N02143.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02143.definitions: a pulse that seems to be yet seems not to be present, like a fish waving in the water

V021-N02144.label: 麻促脉

V021-N02145.label: 卫气营血辨证类

V021-N02147.label: 卫分证

V021-N02149.definitions: categorization of epidemic febrile diseases patterns/ syndromes according to the theory of defense, qi, nutrient and blood which indicate the stages of the clinical course with corresponding pathological changes

V021-N02152.label: 暑入阳明证
V021-N02152.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02152.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of summerheat entering yang brightness
V021-N02152.definitions: a pattern/syndrome attributed to exuberant summerheat that enters yang brightness, i.e., the qi aspect, marked by intense fever, profuse sweating, irritable disposition, headache with dizziness, reddened face, coarse breathing, thirst, dry teeth, yel

V021-N02153.label: 暑兼寒湿证
V021-N02153.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02153.definitions: a pattern/syndrome arising when a combination of summerheat and cold-dampness attacks the exterior of the body, marked by headache, fever, aversion to cold, absence of sweating, epigastric oppression, irritable disposition and thin slimy tongue coatin

V021-N02154.label: 暑湿困阻中焦证
V021-N02154.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02154.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of summerheatdampness encumbering the middle energizer

V021-N02155.label: 暑湿证

V021-N02215.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02215.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of yin deficiency with dryness of the throat
V021-N02215.definitions: a pattern/syndrome marked by a scorching feeling in the throat with itching, mild pain and hoarseness, or sensation of presence of a foreign body in the throat, dry and slightly reddened pharynx or local ulceration, reddened tongue with little moistur

V021-N02216.label: 阴虚鼻窍失濡证
V021-N02216.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02216.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of yin deficiency with dryness of the nose
V021-N02216.definitions: a pattern/syndrome marked by enlarged nasal cavity with feeling of scorching hotness, dried nasal membrane, atrophied conchae and discharge of a little mucus with crusts or blood, or even epistaxis, dry throat and mouth, tidal fever with malar flush,

V021-N02217.label: 风中经络证
V021-N02217.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02217.englishNames: syndrome of channel hit by wind; pattern/syndrome of wind striking the meridians and collaterals; syndrome/pattern of wind striking meridian/channel and collateral
V021-N02217.definitions: a pattern/syndrome marked by numbness, itching or sudden onset of deviation of eye and mouth

V021-N02218.label: 风寒犯头证
V021-N02218.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02218.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of wind-cold invading the head
V021-N02218.definitions: a pattern/syndrome marked by headache with pain of the nape and back, aggravated by wind and cold, thin white tongue coating and floating tight pulse

V021-N02219.label: 风寒犯鼻证
V021-N02219.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02219.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of wind-cold invading the nose
V021-N02219.definitions: a pattern/syndrome marked by stuffy runny nose or nasal itching with sneezing, pale mucous membrane of the nose with thin clear secretion, associated with aversion to wind and cold, thin white tongue coating and floating tight pulse

V021-N02220.label: 风寒袭喉证
V021-N02220.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02220.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of wind-cold assailing the throat
V021-N02220.definitions: a pattern/syndrome marked by painful, itchy and slightly swollen throat dark red in color associated with hoarseness, fever, aversion to cold, absence of sweat, stuffy nose and floating tight pulse

V021-N02221.label: 风寒袭络证
V021-N02221.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02221.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of wind-cold assailing the collaterals
V021-N02221.definitions: a pattern/syndrome marked by aversion to cold, pain and cold feeling of the limbs with contracture or numbness and purple or pale discoloration of the skin, white tongue coating and string-like tight pulse

V021-N02222.label: 风寒阻络证
V021-N02222.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02222.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of wind-cold obstructing the collaterals
V021-N02222.definitions: a pattern/syndrome attributed to impeded blood flow in superficial meridians/channels and collaterals/ networks by pathogenic cold, and manifested by aversion to cold, coldness, pain, contracture or numbness of limbs with dark purple or pale skin, pal

V021-N02223.label: 风湿凌目证
V021-N02223.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02223.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of wind-dampness insulting the eyes
V021-N02223.definitions: a pattern/syndrome marked by swelling of the eyelid, reddened and itchy eye with lacrimination, and photophobia

V021-N02224.label: 风湿犯头证
V021-N02224.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02225.label: 风火攻目证
V021-N02225.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02225.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of wind-fire attacking the eyes
V021-N02225.definitions: a pattern/syndrome marked by reddened, swollen and painful eye and lacrimination associated with fever, aversion to wind and floating rapid pulse

V021-N02226.label: 风热侵喉证
V021-N02226.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02226.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of wind-heat invading the throat
V021-N02226.definitions: a pattern/syndrome marked by painful swollen throat or enlarged congested tonsils, with discomfort in swallowing and hoarseness of voice, fever, slight aversion to wind and cold, mild thirst, thin yellow tongue coating and rapid floating pulse

V021-N02227.label: 风热犯头证
V021-N02227.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02227.definitions: a pattern/syndrome marked by headache with a distention sensation, fever or aversion to wind, thirst, flushing of face, reddened tongue tip and edges, thin and yellowish tongue coating, and rapid floating pulse

V021-N02228.label: 风热犯耳证
V021-N02228.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02228.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of wind-heat invading the ear
V021-N02228.definitions: a pattern/syndrome marked by distension and obstruction in the ear, congestion of the tympanic membrane or a feeling of pressure in the ear, associated with tinnitus, headache, fever with slight aversion to wind and cold, thin yellow tongue coating a

V021-N02229.label: 风热犯鼻证
V021-N02229.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02229.englishNames: pattern/syndrome of wind-heat invading the nose
V021-N02229.definitions: a pattern/syndrome marked by nasal obstruction and discharge, swollen conchae and congested mucous membrane with thick secretion, impairment of the sense of smell, associated with fever and slight aversion to wind and cold, thin yellow tongue coating

V021-N02230.label: 风胜行痹证
V021-N02230.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02230.definitions: a pattern/syndrome attributed to stagnation of pathogenic wind-cold-dampness (with predominance of wind) in sinews, bones and joints, and manifested by wandering arthralgia with inhibited movement of the joint, thin white tongue coating and floating

V021-N02231.label: 四诊
V021-N02231.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02231.definitions: a collective term for inspection, listening and smelling, inquiry, and palpation

V021-N02232.label: 四诊合参
V021-N02232.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02232.definitions: comprehensive consideration of the data obtained from the four examinations (inspection, listening and smelling, inquiry, and palpation) for making the diagnosis

V021-N02233.label: 揆度奇恒
V021-N02233.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02233.englishNames: assessment of the normal and abnormal
V021-N02233.definitions: determining the extent to which an individual is exhibiting abnormal characteristics which may be indicative of a disease or pathological condition

V021-N02234.label: 望诊类
V021-N02234.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02235.label: 三关
V021-N02235.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02235.definitions: a collective term for the three segments of the index finger used for measuring the extension of the visible venules, i.e., “wind bar”, “qi bar” and “life bar”, also known as three gates

V021-N02236.label: 主色
V021-N02236.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02236.definitions: normal natural color of the skin

V021-N02237.label: 便血
V021-N02237.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02237.definitions: loss of blood through anus, with stool

V021-N02238.label: 假神
V021-N02238.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02238.definitions: transient improvement in spirit in a critical case, often indicating approaching death

V021-N02239.label: 光剥舌
V021-N02239.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02239.definitions: a tongue that has lost its normal coating, giving the appearance of having been peeled

V021-N02240.label: 剥苔
V021-N02240.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02240.definitions: complete or partial peeling of the tongue coating

V021-N02241.label: 厚苔
V021-N02241.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02241.definitions: a tongue coating through which the underlying tongue surface is not visible

V021-N02242.label: 吐弄舌
V021-N02242.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02242.definitions: a pathological condition in which the tongue hangs out of the mouth and moves in a circular motion whereby the tip is extended from and retracted back into the mouth, or licks the lips

V021-N02243.label: 命关
V021-N02243.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02243.definitions: the distal segment of the index finger in the inspection of venules, also known as life gate

V021-N02244.label: 善色
V021-N02244.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02244.definitions: bright and lustrous complexion indicating a favorable prognosis, also the same as healthy complexion

V021-N02245.label: 地图舌
V021-N02245.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02245.definitions: a tongue with irregular shedding of the coating and distinct demarcation between the shed and unshed areas, looking like a map

V021-N02246.label: 失神
V021-N02246.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02246.definitions: the general state marked by indifference, low spirit and impaired mental and physical functions with sluggish and inappropriate responses, indicating the existence of a serious pathological condition

V021-N02247.label: 客色
V021-N02247.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02247.definitions: a normal complexion influenced by a change of climate

V021-N02248.label: 少神
V021-N02248.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02248.definitions: the general state marked by indifference, listlessness and low spirit

V021-N02249.label: 强硬舌
V021-N02249.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02249.definitions: a tongue that is stiff, moves sluggishly, and inhibits speech

V021-N02250.label: 得神
V021-N02250.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02250.definitions: the general state marked by high spirit and mental and physical exuberance with prompt and appropriate responses, indicating that the healthy qi is not damaged and improvement from a pathological condition can be expected

V021-N02251.label: 恶色
V021-N02251.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02251.definitions: dark and gloomy complexion indicating an unfavorable prognosis, also the same as unhealthy complexion

V021-N02252.label: 抽搐
V021-N02252.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02252.definitions: sudden, violent uncontrollable movements of the limbs caused by contraction of muscles

V021-N02253.label: 昏厥
V021-N02253.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02253.definitions: sudden loss of consciousness, usually of short duration, sometimes accompanied by cold limbs, also called syncope

V021-N02254.label: 昏蒙
V021-N02254.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02254.definitions: clouding of consciousness with sleepiness, but responsive to calling

V021-N02255.label: 望指纹
V021-N02255.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02255.definitions: a diagnostic method for infants, based on observation of the superficial venules on the palmar side of the index finger

V021-N02256.label: 望神
V021-N02256.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02256.definitions: inspection of the state and vitality, especially mental and physical activities such as consciousness, thinking, facial expression, speech, and response to external stimuli

V021-N02257.label: 望舌
V021-N02257.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02257.definitions: examination of the tongue body and its coating

V021-N02258.label: 望色
V021-N02258.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02258.definitions: observation of the patient’s skin color, particularly the color of the face

V021-N02259.label: 望诊
V021-N02259.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02259.definitions: one of the four diagnostic examinations, including observing the patient’s mental state, facial expression, complexion, physical condition, condition of the tongue, secretions and the distribution of superficial venules of the infant’s fingers

V021-N02260.label: 染苔
V021-N02260.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02260.definitions: a tongue coating that is stained, often by food or medicine

V021-N02261.label: 歪斜舌
V021-N02261.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02261.definitions: a tongue that inclines to one side when extended

V021-N02262.label: 气关
V021-N02262.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02262.definitions: the middle segment of the index finger in the inspection of venules, also known as qi gate

V021-N02263.label: 润苔
V021-N02263.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02264.label: 淡白舌
V021-N02264.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02264.definitions: a tongue less red than normal, indicating qi and blood deficiency or presence of deficiency-cold

V021-N02265.label: 淡红舌
V021-N02265.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02266.label: 滑苔
V021-N02266.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02266.definitions: a moist tongue coating with excessive fluid, feels slippery

V021-N02267.label: 灰苔
V021-N02267.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02267.definitions: tongue coating gray in color with similar clinical significance as black fur

V021-N02268.label: 点刺舌
V021-N02268.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02268.definitions: a tongue with red, white or black spots as well as thorn-like protrusions on its surface

V021-N02269.label: 烦躁
V021-N02269.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02269.definitions: vexation followed by irritability, irascibility, and restlessness

V021-N02270.label: 燥苔
V021-N02270.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02270.definitions: a tongue coating that looks dry and feels dry to the touch

V021-N02271.label: 燥裂苔
V021-N02271.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02271.definitions: a tongue coating that lacks moisture and develops cracks

V021-N02272.label: 病色
V021-N02272.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02272.definitions: abnormal color of the face caused by disease

V021-N02273.label: 痿软舌
V021-N02273.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02273.definitions: a tongue that is flabby and cannot move easily

V021-N02274.label: 瘦薄舌
V021-N02274.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02275.label: 白砂苔
V021-N02275.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02275.definitions: white dry and thick tongue coating like a layer of sand

V021-N02276.label: 白苔
V021-N02276.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02277.label: 真脏色
V021-N02277.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02277.definitions: color reflected in the face indicating exhaustion of the genuine qi of internal organs

V021-N02278.label: 短缩舌
V021-N02278.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02278.definitions: a tongue that cannot be fully extended from the mouth and appears to be contracted

V021-N02279.label: 类剥苔
V021-N02279.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02279.definitions: peeling of the tongue coating leaving an unsmooth tongue surface as if covered with regeneration of coating granules

V021-N02280.label: 粘腻苔
V021-N02280.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02280.definitions: a dense, turbid, slimy tongue coating, covered with a layer of thick turbid mucus

V021-N02281.label: 紫舌
V021-N02281.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02281.definitions: a tongue purple in color, indicating stagnant circulation of qi and blood

V021-N02282.label: 红舌
V021-N02282.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02282.definitions: a tongue redder than normal, indicating presence of heat

V021-N02283.label: 绛舌
V021-N02283.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02283.definitions: a tongue deep red in color, indicating intense heat

V021-N02284.label: 绿苔
V021-N02284.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02285.label: 肌肤甲错
V021-N02285.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02285.definitions: dried, roughened and scaling skin, indicating chronic blood stasis

V021-N02286.label: 肿胀舌
V021-N02286.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02286.definitions: a large and bulging tongue that makes the mouth full or difficult to open

V021-N02287.label: 胕肿
V021-N02287.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02287.definitions: edema associated with pitting of the skin, which resolves only slowly, usually occurring on the instep

V021-N02288.label: 胖大舌
V021-N02288.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02288.definitions: a tongue that is larger than normal, pale in color and delicate, usually bearing dental indentations on the margin

V021-N02289.label: 腐苔
V021-N02289.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02289.definitions: a tongue coating consisting of coarse granules like bean dregs, easy to wipe off

V021-N02290.label: 腻苔
V021-N02290.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02290.definitions: a dense, turbid, slimy tongue coating, sticking on the tongue, hard to wipe off, also known as greasy fur

V021-N02291.label: 舌下络脉
V021-N02291.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02291.definitions: the vein under the tongue on either side of the frenulum

V021-N02292.label: 舌中
V021-N02292.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02292.definitions: the central part of the tongue

V021-N02293.label: 舌体
V021-N02293.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02293.definitions: the musculature and vascular tissue of the tongue, also referring to tongue substance

V021-N02294.label: 舌卷
V021-N02294.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02294.definitions: a tongue formed into a curved shape and drawn back, inhibiting speech

V021-N02295.label: 舌卷囊缩
V021-N02295.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02295.definitions: the formation of the tongue into a curl and the withdrawing of the testicles into the body, seen in critical cases

V021-N02296.label: 舌尖
V021-N02296.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02296.definitions: the anterior end of the tongue

V021-N02297.label: 舌干
V021-N02297.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02297.definitions: a tongue lacking moisture and rough to the touch

V021-N02298.label: 舌形
V021-N02298.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02298.definitions: referring to the shape, luxuriance and witheredness, toughness and softness, thinness and swelling, spots, fissures, and dental indentations

V021-N02299.label: 舌态
V021-N02299.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02299.definitions: the ability of the tongue to move spontaneously

V021-N02300.label: 舌根
V021-N02300.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02300.definitions: the part of the tongue that is attached basally to the bone

V021-N02301.label: 舌神
V021-N02301.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02301.definitions: the general vitality of the tongue manifest in its luxuriance or witheredness

V021-N02302.label: 舌纵
V021-N02302.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02302.definitions: a tongue that is habitually extended out of the mouth and cannot be retracted

V021-N02303.label: 舌色
V021-N02303.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02303.definitions: color of the tongue body, which reflects the condition of qi, blood and the visceral organs

V021-N02304.label: 舌苔
V021-N02304.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02304.definitions: a layer of moss-like material covering the tongue, also called tongue coating

V021-N02305.label: 舌衄
V021-N02305.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02305.definitions: bleeding from the tongue not due to traumatic injury

V021-N02306.label: 舌诊
V021-N02306.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02306.definitions: inspection of the size, shape, color and moisture of the tongue proper and its coating

V021-N02307.label: 舌象

V021-N02310.label: 苔色
V021-N02310.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02313.label: 薄苔
V021-N02313.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02314.label: 裂纹舌
V021-N02314.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02315.label: 谵妄
V021-N02315.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02318.label: 镜面舌

V021-N02319.label: 霉酱苔

V021-N02320.label: 青紫舌

V021-N02323.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02324.label: 面红
V021-N02324.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02325.label: 面色
V021-N02325.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02326.label: 面色晄白

V021-N02327.label: 面色淡白

V021-N02328.label: 面色苍白

V021-N02330.label: 面青

V021-N02332.label: 颤动舌

V021-N02333.label: 风关

V021-N02334.label: 麻痹舌

V021-N02335.label: 黄苔
V021-N02335.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02336.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02336.definitions: tongue coating black in color, indicating either excessive cold or extreme heat in the interior

V021-N02337.label: 齿痕舌

V021-N02339.definitions: a pattern/syndrome marked by bearing-down sensation in the epigastrium and abdomen, protracted diarrhea, even prolapse of rectum or visceroptosis

V021-N02340.label: 寒凝气滞证

V021-N02341.label: 气不摄血证

V021-N02341.definitions: a pattern/syndrome marked by hematochezia, bleeding through the pores, gum bleeding, uterine bleeding or excessive menstrual discharge, listlessness, lack of strength, shortness of breath, laziness to speak, lusterless complexion, pale tongue and weak

V021-N02342.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02342.definitions: a pattern/syndrome that arises when impeded, obstructed or stagnant qi flow impairs the functions of viscera and meridians/channels and is marked by sensation of oppression, distension, and pain associated with frequent sighing, depressed mood and st

V021-N02343.label: 气机失调证

V021-N02343.definitions: a general term for a group of patterns/syndromes attributed to disorders of qi movement including qi stagnation, qi counterflow, qi fall, qi block and qi collapse

V021-N02358.label: 气逆证

V021-N02780.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02781.label: 身热夜甚
V021-N02781.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02781.englishNames: fever worsening at night; fever aggravated at night
V021-N02781.definitions: fever more marked at night than at daytime

V021-N02782.label: 身痒
V021-N02782.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02783.label: 身痛
V021-N02783.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02783.definitions: pain involving the whole body, the same as general body ache

V021-N02784.label: 身重
V021-N02784.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02784.definitions: the subjective heaviness sensation of the body with difficult movement

V021-N02785.label: 遗尿
V021-N02785.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02785.definitions: involuntary discharge of urine during sleep

V021-N02786.label: 郑声
V021-N02786.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02786.definitions: unconscious murmuring haltingly with frequent repetitions

V021-N02787.label: 酸痛
V021-N02787.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02787.definitions: a continuous dull pain as the sensation produced by prolonged physical exertion

V021-N02788.label: 里急
V021-N02788.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02788.definitions: (1) urge to defecate; (2) contraction of male genital organ

V021-N02789.label: 里急后重
V021-N02789.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02789.definitions: ineffectual and painful straining at stool

V021-N02790.label: 重痛
V021-N02790.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02790.definitions: a pain accompanied by heaviness sensation

V021-N02791.label: 错语
V021-N02791.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02791.definitions: a type of dysphasia in which the patient frequently employs wrong words or uses words in incorrect and senseless combinations

V021-N02792.label: 问寒热
V021-N02792.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02792.definitions: asking the patient about sensations of heat and cold

V021-N02793.label: 问汗
V021-N02793.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02793.definitions: asking the patient about his/her condition of sweating

V021-N02794.label: 问诊
V021-N02794.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02794.englishNames: inquiry; listening and smelling examination; inquestions
V021-N02794.definitions: one of the four diagnostic examinations, a way of gaining information for diagnosis by asking the patient about the complaint and the history of the illness

V021-N02795.label: 问诊
V021-N02795.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02795.englishNames: inquiry; listening and smelling examination; inquestions
V021-N02795.definitions: one of the four diagnostic examinations, specifically listening and smelling

V021-N02796.label: 闷痛
V021-N02796.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02796.definitions: a pain accompanied by feeling of oppression

V021-N02797.label: 闻声音
V021-N02797.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02797.definitions: listening to the patient’s voice, sounds of breathing, cough, vomiting, etc. for determining the cold, heat, deficiency or excess nature of disease as a diagnostic method

V021-N02798.label: 阳痿
V021-N02798.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02798.definitions: lack of copulative power in the male

V021-N02799.label: 阴器痛
V021-N02799.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02800.label: 阴汗
V021-N02800.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02800.definitions: localized sweating in the genital region

V021-N02801.label: 阵发痛
V021-N02801.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02802.label: 隐痛
V021-N02802.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02802.definitions: a continuous pain not stabbing in nature

V021-N02803.label: 鞭满
V021-N02803.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02803.definitions: feeling hard to touch with a subjective feeling of fullness

V021-N02804.label: 项强
V021-N02804.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02804.englishNames: stiffness of the neck; stiff neck
V021-N02804.definitions: muscular contraction and rigidity of the neck and the upper back with impaired movement

V021-N02805.label: 项背拘急
V021-N02805.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02805.englishNames: contracture of the nape and neck
V021-N02805.definitions: muscular spasm and rigidity of the nape and back

V021-N02806.label: 飧泄
V021-N02806.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02806.definitions: diarrhea marked by watery stool containing undigested food

V021-N02807.label: 骨蒸
V021-N02807.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02807.definitions: a subjective feeling of fever deep in the body, which appears to emanate from the bone or marrow

V021-N02808.label: 骨蒸潮热
V021-N02808.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02808.definitions: tidal fever in which the heat is felt to emanate from the bones

V021-N02809.label: 骨蒸热
V021-N02809.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02809.definitions: fever which is felt to emanate from the bone or marrow

V021-N02810.label: 鼻不闻香臭
V021-N02810.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02810.definitions: partial or total inability to smell

V021-N02811.label: 鼻塞
V021-N02811.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02812.label: 鼻鼾
V021-N02812.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02812.definitions: rough and noisy breathing while sleeping

V021-N02813.label: 针灸(学)
V021-N02813.parentName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02813.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02814.label: 拔罐类
V021-N02814.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准

V021-N02815.label: 刺络拔罐法
V021-N02815.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02815.definitions: a combined method of pricking and cupping in which pricking with a three-edged needle is followed by cupping to increase bloodletting

V021-N02816.label: 投火法
V021-N02816.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02816.englishNames: fire-insertion cupping method; Cupping after Putting Fire in
V021-N02816.definitions: a cupping procedure which involves inserting a piece of ignited alcohol-cotton or paper into a cup and pressing the cup transversely onto the treated area of the lateral side of the body

V021-N02817.label: 抽气罐
V021-N02817.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02817.definitions: a cup or jar with a rubber valve through which the air inside can be withdrawn by an aspirator

V021-N02818.label: 拔罐
V021-N02818.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02818.definitions: suction by using a vacuumized cup or jar

V021-N02819.label: 拔罐法
V021-N02819.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02819.aliases: 拔罐法; 拔罐子; 负压疗法; 罐具疗法; 火罐气; 角法; 吸筒法
V021-N02819.definitions: a therapeutic method involving the application of suction by placing a vacuumized, usually by fire, cup or jar onto the affected or any part of the body surface

V021-N02820.label: 留罐
V021-N02820.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02820.aliases: 留罐; 留罐法; 坐罐法
V021-N02820.englishNames: retained cupping; Retenting of the Cup
V021-N02820.definitions: a common method of cupping in which the cup or jar is kept at the same site for a certain period of time

V021-N02821.label: 竹罐
V021-N02821.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02821.definitions: a cupping instrument made of bamboo

V021-N02822.label: 药罐
V021-N02822.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02822.definitions: a form of cupping therapy in which the cup or jar is put into boiling medicinal solution before use

V021-N02823.label: 贴棉法
V021-N02823.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02823.definitions: a cupping procedure performed by placing a thin layer of alcohol-cotton on the lower one third of the cup wall, and pressing the cup onto the treated area after igniting the cotton

V021-N02824.label: 走罐
V021-N02824.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02824.definitions: a cupping method in which the cup or jar is moved on the skin surface to extend the acting area

V021-N02825.label: 闪火法
V021-N02825.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02825.definitions: a cupping procedure which involves flashing the fire of a piece of ignited alcohol-cotton once around the cup’s interior and pressing the cup onto the treated area immediately after removing the ignited cotton

V021-N02826.label: 针
V021-N02826.vocabularyName: 传统医学名词术语国际标准
V021-N02826.definitions: the insertion of needles into humans or animals for remedial purposes or its methods
